video:video.video:description: During the second half of Angel City’s home match against the Utah Royals last May 9, King sat on the field in the 74th minute, appearing dazed.

video:video.video:description: Illinois Lt. Gov. Juliana Stratton wins the Democratic primary for U.S. Senate, edging out two sitting members of the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Illinois Lt. Gov Juliana Stratton wins Democratic primary for US Senate
video:video.video:description: Illinois Lt. Gov. Juliana Stratton won Tuesday's Democratic primary for U.S. Senate, edging out two sitting members of the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Behind the scenes of New York's top pleating house
video:video.video:description: Artisan George Kalajian, who crafted the pleats on Lady Gaga’s Super Bowl gown in hours, works a skill that dates before ancient Greece.

video:video.video:title: Gwyneth Paltrow honored at annual New York Women in Film & Television Muse Awards
video:video.video:description: At the New York Women in Film & Television Muse Awards held Friday in New York City, honoree Gwyneth Paltrow says it's important for women to come together and support one another.

video:video.video:title: Images released by Royal Thai Navy shows Thai cargo ship set ablaze in Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: Authorities in Thailand have released images of the Thai cargo ship that was struck and set ablaze in the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Smoke rises after airstrikes in various parts of Iran's capital Tehran
video:video.video:description: Plumes of smoke were seen rising across various parts of Tehran on Thursday morning as U.S.-Israeli airstrikes continued to pound the Iranian capital.

video:video.video:title: War on Iran boils down to a single question: Who can take the pain the longest? AP explains
video:video.video:description: The ongoing Israeli-American war on Iran, for all its complexity and global effects, boils down to a single question: Who can take the pain the longest?

video:video.video:title: 'Imperfect Women' cast celebrates imperfections
video:video.video:description: Kerry Washington, Elisabeth Moss and Kate Mara, who take the lead roles in new thriller 'Imperfect Women,' are willing to tell the "tooth" about their own imperfections.

video:video.video:title: Kenya experiments with giving government workers menstrual leave
video:video.video:description: It started with a casual lunch conversation between a county governor and his cabinet ministers about a colleague’s menstrual pain.

video:video.video:title: Motorists queue for petrol in Bangladesh, amid fears Iran war could spark serious fuel shortage
video:video.video:description: Many even waited overnight in many fuel stations in Dhaka, the capital, to get fuel under a government rationing system allowing cars and bikes to collect a limited amount of fuel.

video:video.video:title: Residents in southern Israel town of Arad are shocked after overnight strike by Iran
video:video.video:description: Residents in the southern town of Arad reacted with shock after an overnight Iranian strike wounded dozens of people and caused heavy damage to several buildings.

video:video.video:title: A US strike on a suspected drug boat off Costa Rica leaves 2 dead, 1 critically hurt
video:video.video:description: A U.S. strike on a boat suspected of transporting illegal drugs along Costa Rica's Pacific coast left at least two people dead and one seriously injured, Costa Rican authorities said Friday.

video:video.video:title: Islamic leaders in Michigan denounce Iran war, call for immediate ceasefire
video:video.video:description: A group of Michigan Islamic religious and community leaders denounced U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran and called for an immediate end to hostilities in the Middle East (AP video: Mike Householder)

video:video.video:title: Quinta Brunson and Bowen Yang honored at 2026 GLAAD Awards
video:video.video:description: Quinta Brunson, Bowen Yang and Matt Rogers were honored at the 2026 GLAAD Awards with Brunson receiving the GLAAD Vanguard Award and Yang and Rogers the Stephen F.

video:video.video:title: At least 6 killed, 37 injured in Ukrainian attack on Russia’s Bryansk
video:video.video:description: At least six people were killed and 37 were injured in a Ukrainian missile attack on Russia’s Bryansk region, local governor Alexander Bogomaz said in a post on social media.

video:video.video:title: U.S.-Israel strikes on Iran threaten to worsen inflation and slow the economy
video:video.video:description: Oil prices jumped Tuesday for the second straight day and gas prices moved higher in the United States, underscoring the threat of rising inflation posed by the Iran war.

video:video.video:title: Azerbaijan president accuses neighbor Iran of drone attack, calls it 'heinous terrorist act'
video:video.video:description: Azerbaijan on Thursday accused Iran of a drone attack on its territory that injured four civilians, and it vowed to retaliate in a sign that the war in the Middle East was spreading.

video:video.video:title: People run for cover in Tel Aviv as missiles fired from Lebanon
video:video.video:description: The military said that "the Israeli Air Force intercepted several launches and several additional launches fell in an open area.

video:video.video:title: California Gov. Newsom still ‘processing’ César Chavez allegations
video:video.video:description: Labor rights activist Dolores Huerta revealed she was among women and girls who say they were sexually abused by César Chavez, the widely admired Latino icon who brought to light the struggles of farmhands while leading the United Farm Workers union.

video:video.video:title: Italian tourist stranded in Dubai leaves UAE via Oman to return to Rome
video:video.video:description: An Italian tourist stranded in Dubai due to the US-Israeli strikes on Iran and Tehran's subsequent retaliation, left the UAE on a bus bound for Oman on Tuesday, organized by the Italian Ministry of Foreign Affairs.

video:video.video:title: Family members of a man suspected of attacking a Michigan synagogue were killed in an Israeli airstrike last week, AP source says
video:video.video:description: Ayman Mohamad Ghazali lost two of his brothers, a niece and a nephew during an Israeli airstrike in Lebanon, an official said.

video:video.video:title: Powerful storm chain brings blizzard conditions to Wisconsin
video:video.video:description: Warnings of hazardous road conditions were issued across Minnesota, Michigan and Wisconsin, where transportation officials warned of worsening conditions Sunday with low visibility and snow-covered roadways.

video:video.video:title: Smoke seen rising from warehouses near Irbil airport in Iraq's northern Kurdish region
video:video.video:description: Smoke from a suspected drone attack was seen rising from warehouses belonging to various international oil and construction companies near Irbil International Airport in Iraq on Saturday.

video:video.video:title: Israeli drone strike hits hotel in central Beirut - AP Explains
video:video.video:description: Israel says it targeted Iranian-linked commanders in Beirut after a strike hit the Ramada Hotel, popular with tourists and business travelers, and was heard by residents nearby.

video:video.video:title: 'Smartphone for pets' lets furry friends call their owners
video:video.video:description: Thought it was just humans that used smartphones? Think again. At the Barcelona mobile fair MWC, a Singaporean company is showing off a "smartphone for pets" that lets furry friends call their owners.

video:video.video:title: Aftermath of strike that hit village near Sidon, Lebanon
video:video.video:description: A strike early Tuesday morning hit a village near the coastal city of Sidon. There were no immediate reports of casualties from the Israeli airstrike, but the Israeli military issued a warning before the strike and said Hezbollah was using the building without giving further details.

video:video.video:title: Travellers from Dubai reunite with family and friends as they arrive in Lisbon on commerical flight
video:video.video:description: The first flight departing from Dubai to Lisbon since the beginning of the Iran war arrived on Wednesday night carrying around 300 passengers.

video:video.video:title: Trump encourages Latin American leaders to use military action to help US fight cartels
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Saturday that the U.S. and Latin American countries were banding together to combat violent cartels.

video:video.video:title: Israeli strikes continue to hit Lebanon, including areas in central Beirut and its southern suburbs
video:video.video:description: In renewed fighting with the Lebanese militant group Hezbollah, Israel has concentrated most strikes where the group has a strong presence in southern Lebanon and Beirut’s southern suburbs.

video:video.video:title: Worshippers gather in Kyiv to pay respects to the late Patriarch Filaret
video:video.video:description: Worshippers in Kyiv gathered at St. Michael's Golden-Domed Monastery on Friday to pay their respects to the late Patriarch Emeritus Filaret, one of the leaders of the Orthodox Church of Ukraine.

video:video.video:title: Clashes at World Water Day protest in Chile over President Kast's rollback of environmental rules
video:video.video:description: Thousands of people marched on Sunday in Santiago and other cities in Chile to mark World Water Day following the withdrawal of dozens of environmental decrees by arch-conservative President José Antonio Kast.

video:video.video:title: For airline travelers, the shutdown answer is simple: Pay TSA officers
video:video.video:description: Regardless of politics or destination, American air travelers were unified by one desire Saturday: It's time to pay Transportation Security Administration employees.

video:video.video:title: Chase Infiniti looks back on 'One Battle After Another'
video:video.video:description: Chase Infiniti says her love for “One Battle After Another” can’t be shaken by any outside opinions.

video:video.video:title: Major seaside tourist area of Beirut hit by an Israeli strike
video:video.video:description: An Israeli strike hit a car Thursday in Ramlet al-Bayda, a major seaside tourist area of Beirut where dozens of displaced people have been sheltering.

video:video.video:title: Putin says European leaders have only themselves to blame for their energy crisis
video:video.video:description: Russia's Vladimir Putin said on Wednesday that European governments had only themselves to blame for their energy predicament.

video:video.video:title: Explosions light up the sky following airstrikes on western Tehran
video:video.video:description: Several explosions lit up the sky in western Tehran as Israeli warplanes pounded the Iranian capital in the early hours of Saturday.

video:video.video:title: Seven killed and 21 wounded in Israeli strike on central Beirut
video:video.video:description: The Lebanese Health Ministry said an Israeli strike killed seven people and wounded 21 in the coastal area of Ramlet al-Bayda in central Beirut, early on Thursday.

video:video.video:title: Firefighters and rescuers dispatched to site of Iranian missile hit in Central Israel
video:video.video:description: Firefighters and rescuers were dispatched to the location of an Iranian missile hit in an open area in Central Israel on Friday. (AP video by Sam Mednick)

video:video.video:title: Large explosion hits Lebanese capital Beirut
video:video.video:description: A large explosion hit the Lebanese capital, Beirut, on Tuesday as Israel’s air force struck a southern suburb.

video:video.video:title: US and Israel have ‘superiority’ and control nearly all Iran's airspace, Israeli diplomat says
video:video.video:description: Israeli ambassador Danny Danon told reporters at the United Nations "we will be able to show that superiority in the next few days" and he reported that they have control of almost all of Iran's airspace.

video:video.video:title: Iranian family in Indiana celebrates Nowruz, worries about loved ones in Iran
video:video.video:description: Nahid Rezaei and her husband, Farman Moayed, are celebrating Nowruz in their home in a suburb of Indianapolis.

video:video.video:title: US traveler documents getting caught in the Middle East while going to wedding in India
video:video.video:description: Explosions in the sky woke Cory McKane in Dubai on Saturday, turning a quick visit before a friend’s wedding in India into a tense scramble to escape the Middle East as the Iran war expanded.

video:video.video:title: Bodies of two girls found buried in suitcases in Cleveland
video:video.video:description: Authorities in Cleveland are working to identify two young half-sisters whose bodies were found buried inside suitcases on the city’s east side.

video:video.video:title: Three newborn lion cubs unveiled at German zoo
video:video.video:description: Three Asian lion cubs were unveiled at Cologne Zoo on Thursday. The cubs, born at the zoo at the end of November, were seen exploring their outdoor enclosure for the first time.

video:video.video:title: Bomb squad responds to a 'suspicious device' outside NYC mayor's house
video:video.video:description: Police officers responded on Sunday to a "suspicious device" which had been found in Manhattan's Upper East Side.

video:video.video:title: Trump says the attack on Iran 'was our last, best chance to strike'
video:video.video:description: With no sign of the conflict abating anytime soon, President Donald Trump says that the military operations are likely to last four to five weeks but that he was prepared “to go far longer than that.”

video:video.video:title: Brazil's telenovela industry is the secret agent behind powerful films at the Oscars
video:video.video:description: Hollywood actors might rule the silver screen — as Sunday’s Academy Awards is poised to prove — but Brazil’s path to stardom often starts under the bright lights of a TV studio rather than a sprawling movie set.

video:video.video:title: How Netflix is bringing BTS's Gwanghwamun comeback to the world
video:video.video:description: Netflix is pulling out all the stops for BTS’s comeback, deploying more than 1,000 crew members and 25 cameras - including rare government approved drones - to broadcast the show live from Seoul’s historic Gwanghwamun Square. (20 March)

video:video.video:title: One dead in Ukrainian drone attack on Russia’s Krasnodar region
video:video.video:description: A Ukrainian drone hit an apartment building in the southern Russian city of Krasnodar, killing one resident, according to the city’s head Yevgeny Naumov.

video:video.video:title: Britney Spears arrested for DUI
video:video.video:description: Authorities say Britney Spears was pulled over, after reports that her BMW was driving fast and erratically, on a highway Wednesday night and jailed after taking a series of field sobriety tests.

video:video.video:title: US drivers lament the spike in gas prices
video:video.video:description: Drivers speak out after the average price for a gallon of gasoline jumped 11 cents overnight in the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Former ambassadors say US not using its Middle East expertise in Iran war
video:video.video:description: The widening war in Iran is exposing big gaps at the State Department, according to former U.S. ambassadors of Middle Eastern countries who say the expertise they and other career diplomats could provide is going unheard.

video:video.video:title: Police responds to scene after armed man rams vehicle into Michigan synagogue
video:video.video:description: A man armed with a rifle rammed a vehicle into a major synagogue in a Detroit suburb and was fatally shot by security.

video:video.video:title: UN expresses alarm about the 'multiplication of new fronts' in the Middle East
video:video.video:description: Israel sent troops into southern Lebanon on Tuesday and warned residents of more than 80 villages to evacuate as the Iran-backed militant Hezbollah group said it was ready for an “open war” with Israel in the wake of the U.S.

video:video.video:title: AP Reporter at site of Iranian strike in Central Israel
video:video.video:description: AP Reporter Sam Mednick was in central Israel at a site where an Iranian missile struck Friday. This came as Israel and the US continued their attacks on Iran.

video:video.video:title: Friend of ROTC leader killed in Old Dominion University shooting visits campus to honor him
video:video.video:description: Eddie Flack expressed his respect and appreciation for the men and women who serve in the military to protect our country, “It's just unfortunate that they have to fight a two-front war, domestic and abroad.”

video:video.video:title: Mexican Navy seizes nearly 2 tons of suspected cocaine at sea as US continues lethal strikes
video:video.video:description: Mexican authorities Monday released images showing its Navy seizing packages of suspected cocaine during a maritime operation in the Pacific Ocean Friday.

video:video.video:title: First government evacuation plane repatriating Czechs from Middle East lands in Prague
video:video.video:description: The first military repatriation flight for Czechs stranded in the Middle East landed in Prague shortly before 9 p.m. (2000GMT)on Tuesday.

video:video.video:description: The Israeli military on Saturday reported that it had struck Mehrabad Airport in Tehran that it alleged was used to transport weapons to militant groups that Iran supports across the Middle East.

video:video.video:title: Protesters in Slovenia unsuccessfully approach US embassy, urge voting in parliamentary elections
video:video.video:description: Left-wing protesters gathered in the Slovenian capital Ljubljana on Friday, calling on voters to take part in Sunday’s parliamentary elections and warning against foreign interference in the campaign.

video:video.video:title: Autumn Durald Arkapaw becomes first woman to win Oscar for cinematography
video:video.video:description: Autumn Durald Arkapaw became the first woman to win an Academy Award for best cinematography. She was recognized for her work on Ryan Coogler’s “Sinners.”

video:video.video:title: Venezuela government supporters holds a small solidarity march for Iran
video:video.video:description: Dozens of Venezuelan government supporters on Tuesday marched in the capital in solidarity with Iran and mourning Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed on the first day of the war.

video:video.video:title: Tens of thousands in Tbilisi mourn Georgia’s late Patriarch Ilia II
video:video.video:description: Tens of thousands of people took part in a procession in Tbilisi on Wednesday, accompanying the coffin of Georgia’s Patriarch to the Holy Trinity Cathedral.

video:video.video:title: Reported projectile strike at Iran's Bushehr nuclear power complex raises concerns, AP explains
video:video.video:description: In Iran, the Bushehr nuclear power plant complex was hit by a projectile on Tuesday evening but there were no injuries and the plant suffered no damage, the International Atomic Energy Agency said after receiving a report from Tehran.

video:video.video:title: Brazilian police operation in Rio favelas leaves at least 8 dead, including local drug boss
video:video.video:description: A police operation in Rio de Janeiro killed seven suspected traffickers, including a gang leader, as well as a resident caught in the crossfire.

video:video.video:description: Andreas Kronthaler presented his latest collection for Vivienne Westwood in Paris, inspired by the works of Italian costume designer Danilo Donati and actress Romy Schneider.

video:video.video:title: Attacks targeted oil ship near southern port in Basra
video:video.video:description: An attack on Iraq’s Basra port early Thursday killed at least one person and forced authorities to halt operations at all the country’s oil terminals, officials said.

video:video.video:title: Interception and smoke trails seen above morning sky in Dubai
video:video.video:description: Smoke was seen rising into the sky above Dubai on Sunday after flights across the Middle East were disrupted, and air defense fire thudded over the United Arab Emirates’ commercial capital.

video:video.video:description: Cherry blossom spotters from the Japanese government announced Thursday the blooming of the country’s favourite flower in Tokyo, marking the official start of the season.

video:video.video:description: A U.S. submarine sank an Iranian warship in the Indian Ocean, as Washington and Israel intensified their bombardment Wednesday of Iran's security forces and other symbols of power.

video:video.video:description: Texas Sen. John Cornyn and Attorney General Ken Paxton will compete in a May runoff for the Republican nomination for U.S. Senate.

video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump erroneously claimed Monday that Iran has access to the American Tomahawk cruise missile, the weapon likely used to strike a girls school in Iran, killing 165 people.

video:video.video:title: Israel targets Hezbollah leaders in Beirut airstrike
video:video.video:description: Israel's military on Monday said it had carried out a “targeted attack” on senior members of Lebanese militant group Hezbollah in Beirut and southern Lebanon.

video:video.video:title: Atlanta celebrates St. Patrick's day with a parade
video:video.video:description: Over 2,000 people participated in a St. Patrick’s Day parade in Atlanta on Saturday. The parade was part of boisterous celebrations being held in cities across the US.

video:video.video:title: Ukrainian negotiators are in America and talking to US officials in bid to end war, Zelenskyy says
video:video.video:description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said on Saturday that a Ukrainian team had met US Special Envoy Steve Witkoff and U.S.

video:video.video:title: Strikes in Beirut send plumes of smoke into the air
video:video.video:description: Strikes in Beirut send plumes of smoke into the air

video:video.video:title: Kazakhstanis vote in referendum on new constitution
video:video.video:description: The referendum puts to a vote a new constitution that would strengthen President Kassym-Jomart Tokayev’s grip on power in Central Asia’s largest country.

video:video.video:title: Taliban leaders hold funerals for some of at least 408 killed in airstrike
video:video.video:description: Taliban leaders held funerals in Kabul for those killed in an airstrike earlier in the week. Afghanistan accused Pakistan of killing at least 408 people in an airstrike on the drug rehabilitation hospital in the Afghan capital late Monday.

video:video.video:title: Video appears to show New York City bomb suspect buying fuse at a fireworks store
video:video.video:description: Newly released surveillance video appears to show a man buying a fuse at a fireworks store days before authorities say he and another man brought homemade bombs to a protest outside the New York City mayor’s residence.

video:video.video:title: Teyana Taylor walks Actor Awards carpet with daughter
video:video.video:description: Teyana Taylor's daughter Rue poses alongside her mother on the Actor Awards red carpet.

video:video.video:title: People line up for ice cream treats every March 1 at this Minnesota Dairy Queen. Why? It's tradition
video:video.video:description: It might still be winter in Minnesota, but spring is in the air for people lined up at a Dairy Queen in Moorhead, Minnesota, for its annual opening.

video:video.video:title: Body of American student who went missing in Barcelona has been found
video:video.video:description: The body of James “Jimmy” Gracey, a college student who went missing while in Spain on spring break, was found Thursday in the water off a Barcelona beach, police said.

video:video.video:title: Pro-Iran protesters clash with police near Green Zone in Baghdad
video:video.video:description: Iraqi security forces fired teargas at dozens of protesters on Sunday evening after they attempted to enter Baghdad's heavily-fortified Green Zone, which hosts the U.S. embassy.

video:video.video:title: Controlled explosions carried out to prevent ice jams on Russian rivers and reduce spring floods
video:video.video:description: A military engineering team of the Novosibirsk missile formation carried out controlled explosions Friday on a tributary of the Ob River in Russia's Siberia to prevent the formation of ice jams and reduce the risk of spring flooding.

video:video.video:title: On heels of attempted attack, Mamdani says 'extremism of any kind will not be tolerated' in NYC
video:video.video:description: Authorities said counterprotesters allegedly attempted to detonate two improvised explosive devices during a protest near the mayor's home over the weekend.

video:video.video:title: Hawaii's fragrant flower lei face competition from cheaper imports
video:video.video:description: Some Hawaii lawmakers think the state should do more to help producers of lei made with locally grown, fragrant flowers, rather than imported purple orchids, but some lei sellers worry that proposed rules would make the garlands too expensive.

video:video.video:title: Pentagon policy chief says US and Israel have 'overlapping' objectives in Iran
video:video.video:description: U.S. Under Secretary of Defense for Policy Elbridge Colby said Wednesday that the U.S. and Israel have "overlapping" objectives for the war in Iran, with the U.S. military focusing on degrading and destroying Iran's ability to project military power.

video:video.video:title: Iranian president says new leadership council ‘has begun its work’ after death of supreme leader
video:video.video:description: Pezeshkian made the comment in a prerecorded message aired on Iranian state television. He is one of three officials on the council.

video:video.video:title: Black smoke billows into Beirut sky after explosion as Israel continues to launch strikes
video:video.video:description: Israel renewed its assault on southern Lebanon early Sunday, including targeting commanders of the Lebanese branch of Iran’s Revolutionary Guards’ Quds Force, after Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu promised “many surprises” for the next phase of the conflict.

video:video.video:title: 'The Madison' felt 'familiar' for Matthew Fox
video:video.video:description: Matthew Fox says shooting Taylor Sheridan's new series "The Madison" felt "very familiar" after growing up on a Wyoming ranch. (March 9)

video:video.video:title: Major fire at Glasgow building closes Scotland's busiest train station
video:video.video:description: Glasgow Central railway station was forced to close Monday following a major blaze at a nearby vape shop, with travel disruption expected throughout the day.

video:video.video:title: New study finds ultrasound could help save hedgehogs from road deaths
video:video.video:description: Could ultrasonic hearing save hedgehogs from becoming roadkill? New research shows they can hear noise at frequencies as high as 85 kilohertz, raising the prospect of specially-designed sound repellers to alert them to approaching vehicles.

video:video.video:title: Fake images spread online as conflict in Middle East widens
video:video.video:description: Fake images spread online as conflict in Middle East widens

video:video.video:title: Paraguay becomes final South American country to approve Mercosur-European Union trade deal
video:video.video:description: Paraguay became the latest—and final—South American country to ratify the long-awaited trade agreement between Mercosur and the European Union on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Jessie Buckley won her first Oscar, and revealed her daughter got her first tooth
video:video.video:description: Jessie Buckley took home an Oscar for Best Actress at Sunday's Academy Awards for her role in "Hamnet."

video:video.video:title: Robert Pattinson on working with Zendaya in multiple films
video:video.video:description: Robert Pattinson suspects Zendaya may have put in a good word for him with “Dune” franchise director Denis Villeneuve.

video:video.video:title: Plane lands at Heathrow airport bringing passengers from Abu Dhabi
video:video.video:description: Some British tourists who were stranded in the United Arab Emirates were relieved to land safely in London's Heathrow Airport on Monday night.

video:video.video:title: Ryan Coogler 'not thinking about' potential Oscars history
video:video.video:description: Ryan Coogler shares what he is focused on heading into Oscar Sunday. His film "Sinners" has an historic 16 nominations.

video:video.video:title: Top US commander in Middle East says strikes on Iranian navy have ‘intensified’
video:video.video:description: Speaking at U.S. Central Command, which oversees the Middle East, Hegseth said the U.S. is solely controlling the timeline of activities in Iran and will do so “as long as it takes to ensure the United States of America achieves” its objectives.

video:video.video:title: Oil and gas prices rise in US as Iran digs in with new leader
video:video.video:description: The price of oil surged higher and showed no signs of halting its rapid climb a week after the U.S. and Israel launched major attacks on Iran that escalated into a war in the Middle East.

video:video.video:title: Europe rallies around Cyprus during Iran war as Macron visits to show support for island
video:video.video:description: French President Emmanuel Macron on Monday pledged to defend Cyprus, days after dispatching a warship to the east Mediterranean island nation, where a Shahed drone struck a British air base on its southern coast last week during the Iran war.

video:video.video:title: Tehran intensifies attacks on Gulf energy facilities after Israel hits Iranian gas field
video:video.video:description: Iran intensified its attacks on oil and natural gas facilities around the Gulf on Thursday, raising the stakes in a war that is sending shock waves through the global economy.

video:video.video:title: Midterm primary elections kick off amid Iran conflict, voters express frustration in Arkansas
video:video.video:description: Arkansas voters will choose nominees for a full slate of federal, state and local offices in primary elections Tuesday. (AP Video shot by Adrian Sainz)

video:video.video:title: Crew members rescued from stricken Thai cargo ship return to Bangkok
video:video.video:description: Rescued crew members from a Thai cargo ship that was struck and set ablaze near the Strait of Hormuz have arrived in Bangkok from Oman.

video:video.video:title: AP Explains: Mideast war shakes global energy markets as oil and gas prices surge
video:video.video:description: Oil prices rose sharply Monday as disruptions to tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz raised uncertainty about how U.S. and Israeli attacks on Iran would affect supply to the world economy.

video:video.video:title: Iraqis prepare for Eid al Fitr amid economic crisis and Iran war
video:video.video:description: Iraqis crowded in a Baghdad market on Wednesday as they shopped and prepared for the Muslim holiday of Eid al-Fitr amid an economic crisis triggered by the war in Iran.

video:video.video:title: Trump: Not yet 'ready to leave' Iran but predicts an exit in the 'very near future'
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Tuesday he is "not ready to leave" Iran yet, but vowed an exit "in the very near future."

video:video.video:title: Sri Lanka says 32 crew members were rescued, while 87 bodies recovered from Iranian ship sunk by US
video:video.video:description: Sailors aboard the Iranian warship that was struck by a U.S.-fired torpedo off the coast of Sri Lanka were being treated in hospital on Thursday.

video:video.video:title: A taste of Ireland — delivered: Mobile pubs roll into New England neighborhoods
video:video.video:description: Two Massachusetts brothers have built a fleet of tiny Irish pubs on wheels — miniature bars they tow to backyards, weddings and neighborhood parties across New England.

video:video.video:title: Fire breaks out after drone interception in UAE
video:video.video:description: A fire broke out Saturday in Fujairah in the United Arab Emirates following an interception of a drone, authorities said.

video:video.video:title: What to know about Holi, the Hindu festival of colors
video:video.video:description: Holi, widely known as the Hindu festival of colors, is a joyful annual celebration at the advent of spring with cultural and religious significance.

video:video.video:title: Mississippi voters discuss the issues behind their primary choices
video:video.video:description: Mississippi voters will choose party nominees for Congress in a state primary Tuesday. Voters in Jackson talked about the issues they care about most as they arrived at the polls.

video:video.video:title: Tons of aid flow into Cuba as humanitarian convoy arrives on the struggling island
video:video.video:description: Some 650 delegates from 33 countries and 120 organizations began arriving in Cuba on Friday as part of a solidarity caravan transporting some 20 tons of humanitarian aid as the island grapples with a severe energy crisis.

video:video.video:title: Drone strike kills UN aid worker in the eastern Congo city of Goma
video:video.video:description: A series of explosions attributed to drones killed at least one person, a U.N. aid worker, as the attack on the Wednesday rocked downtown Goma in eastern Congo, according to the M23 rebel group and local residents.

video:video.video:title: Israeli airstrikes target Iran’s presidential office and facilities linked to Supreme Leader
video:video.video:description: Israel carried out airstrikes targeting Iran’s presidential office and facilities linked to the Supreme Leader on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: AP explains: Iran war causes growing pressure on oil production in region
video:video.video:description: Iran has effectively stopped tankers from using the Strait of Hormuz, which connects the Persian Gulf and the Gulf of Oman — the gateway to the Indian Ocean — through which 20% of the world's oil is carried.

video:video.video:title: Odessa A'zion reveals why two of her songs disappeared from streaming platforms
video:video.video:description: Odessa A'zion says she hasn't had time to release new music after starring in "Marty Supreme" and "I Love LA."

video:video.video:title: Kurdish Nowruz festival lights up Akra with torches and fireworks
video:video.video:description: Thousands of Kurds celebrated the new year festival of Nowruz in the hub city of Akra in Iraq's autonomous Kurdish region.

video:video.video:title: UK Prime Minister says Britain 'will not be drawn into the wider war' in Gulf
video:video.video:description: Keir Starmer spoke after U.S. President Donald Trump said he’d demanded U.S. allies send warships to open the key oil shipment route.

video:video.video:title: Members of the UN peacekeeping force in Lebanon call for cessation of hostilities
video:video.video:description: Contributing countries to the UN peacekeeping force in Lebanon, were joined by other member states on Wednesday, to express deep alarm at the escalation of hostilities in Lebanon.

video:video.video:title: Queues at petrol stations in Vietnam as oil prices soar
video:video.video:description: Vietnam raised petrol prices to 27,040 dong ($1.10) per liter on Saturday, a 22% increase from the previous price.

video:video.video:title: Debris sent flying as suspected tornado strikes southwest Michigan
video:video.video:description: A suspected tornado hit Three Rivers, Michigan on Friday, as powerful storms ripped across the state.

video:video.video:title: Kamala Harris eulogizes Jackson as a leader who fought barriers to 'opportunity, justice and freedom'
video:video.video:description: Kamala Harris eulogizes Jackson as a leader who fought barriers to 'opportunity, justice and freedom'

video:video.video:title: Emmanuel Grégoire becomes new mayor of Paris, succeeding fellow Socialist Anne Hidalgo
video:video.video:description: Socialist candidate Emmanuel Grégoire won the Paris mayoral race Sunday, succeeding fellow party member Anne Hidalgo as the French capital’s mayor.

video:video.video:title: Teyana Taylor's One Fabulous Look After Another
video:video.video:description: Teyana Taylor has been a standout on the red carpet this awards season, even bringing her young daughter to help style her at the Actor Awards.

video:video.video:title: Hometown Halfpipe Hero: Eileen Gu leads San Francisco’s Chinese New Year Parade
video:video.video:description: Olympic freestyle skiing champion Eileen Gu returned to her hometown of San Francisco as Grand Marshal of the San Francisco Chinese New Year Parade, receiving a warm welcome from crowds despite past controversy over her decision to compete for China in the Olympics instead of the United States.

video:video.video:title: Thunderstorm lashes Pakistan’s largest city, Karachi, causing deaths
video:video.video:description: A thunderstorm and strong winds killed 16 people in Pakistan’s largest city, Karachi, a rescue worker said Wednesday evening. (AP Video shot by Muhammad Farooq)

video:video.video:title: Iranian missiles intercepted above Doha
video:video.video:description: Iranian missiles could be seen being intercepted above Doha on Saturday night into Sunday morning.

video:video.video:title: How Pfeiffer and Russell worked on 'The Madison' through scheduling challenges
video:video.video:description: Stars Michelle Pfeiffer and Kurt Russell discuss making Taylor Sheridan's latest show "The Madison," at its premiere in New York. (March 10)

video:video.video:title: Cuba is gradually recovering from an islandwide blackout, but millions are still without electricity
video:video.video:description: Energy is gradually being restored after Monday’s nationwide power outage, but millions remain without electricity. At this stage, Cuba is running on fumes.

video:video.video:description: Venezuela's acting president Delcy Rodriguez received the trophy of the World Baseball Classic Venezuela won against the U.S. on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: AP explains: How Iran war is driving fast-rising oil prices
video:video.video:description: Global energy trade is in turmoil as war around the Persian Gulf chokes off oil and natural gas shipments, causing prices to soar.

video:video.video:title: Fire at South Korean auto parts factory injures over 50
video:video.video:description: South Korean officials say at least 55 people were injured in a fire at an auto parts factory in the central city of Daejeon.

video:video.video:description: Saja Kilani, a star of the docudrama “The Voice of Hind Rajab,” wears an Artists4Ceasefire pin on the Oscars red carpet.

video:video.video:title: What to know about the war in the Middle East
video:video.video:description: The intensity of the attacks, the killing of Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, and the lack of any apparent exit plan indicated the conflict would not end anytime soon.

video:video.video:title: The faces of BTS light up night sky in Seoul, in drone display
video:video.video:description: Thousands of drones create portraits of the seven members of BTS, in a light show watched by hundreds of fans on the banks of the river Han, Friday night, ahead of the K-pop superstars' hotly anticipated comeback concert.

video:video.video:description: Associated Press journalist Juwon Park documents her journey to BTS' first show in Seoul after 4-year hiatus. (Mar. 21)

video:video.video:description: U.S. Olympic gymnast Jordan Chiles’ last home meet at UCLA was full of team spirit, tears and celebrity appearances.

video:video.video:title: Greenland's sea ice is melting. Fishermen worry what comes next
video:video.video:description: Greenland’s fishermen are struggling as warming weather from climate change makes the sea ice unreliable and the fish harder to predict.

video:video.video:title: Millions of people in India celebrate colorful Holi festival
video:video.video:description: Millions of people in India celebrated the colorful Holi festival on Wednesday. Thousands danced and got soaked in colored water and powder at the Swaminarayan Temple, reveling in traditional Gujarati music.

video:video.video:title: Iranian President rejects US demand for an unconditional surrender
video:video.video:description: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian also apologized on Saturday for Iran's attacks on neighbouring countries, suggesting it was the result of miscommunication in the ranks and insisted that it would stop from now on.

video:video.video:title: California officials downplay information that Iran may be targeting their state with drone strikes
video:video.video:description: California officials say they are aware of information in various news reports that Iran could be targeting the U.S. with drone strikes in retaliation for the ongoing war in Iran but many are downplaying that threat.

video:video.video:title: US Embassy in Saudi Arabia hit with Iranian drones as American and Israeli attacks on Iran escalate
video:video.video:description: Iran hit the U.S. Embassy in Saudi Arabia’s capital with a drone strike early Tuesday as it kept striking targets around the region, while the United States and Israel pounded Iran with airstrikes.

video:video.video:title: England soccer star Chloe Kelly celebrated with Barbie
video:video.video:description: Lioness Chloe Kelly becomes the first soccer player to have a Barbie doll made in her likeness, as part of the brand's Dream Team collection celebrating women. (March 5)

video:video.video:title: Iran war has US farmers worried about the cost and availability of fertilizer
video:video.video:description: Rising fertilizer prices and potential shortages from shipping disruptions due to the Iran war have farmers and U.S. agriculture groups bracing ahead of the spring planting season, raising costs for farmers and potentially pushing some out of business.

video:video.video:title: Stella McCartney lets horses steal the show at Paris Fashion Week
video:video.video:description: Stella McCartney staged her Fall 2026 show Monday inside a riding hall, west of Paris, marking the Lunar New Year of the Horse with what amounted to a quiet manifesto: fashion can celebrate animals rather than consume them.

video:video.video:title: US Defense Department video appears to show US forces hitting Iranian mobile launchers
video:video.video:description: The video release comes as the United States and Israel stepped up airstrikes on Iranian missile launchers and factories Tuesday.

video:video.video:description: Thousands of Syrians have crossed from Lebanon into Syria to flee Israeli strikes over the past two days as Israel and the Iran-allied Lebanese militant group Hezbollah escalated their attacks against each other.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories March 17
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Tuesday, March 17th: Trump says an ex-president said he wishes he had attacked Iran; Explosions heard in Dubai and Qatar; Island-wide blackout in Cuba; Utah woman who wrote book on grief convicted of killing husband.

video:video.video:description: Major storms whipped up tornadoes that killed at least two people in northwest Indiana and leveled buildings in Kankakee, Illinois.

video:video.video:title: Oscar-nominated directors highlight workers at a women's health clinic in 'The Devil Is Busy'
video:video.video:description: The Oscar-nominated documentary short, "The Devil is Busy," focuses on a day in the life of a women's health clinic in Georgia, where women's reproductive rights have been strictly limited in the U.S.

video:video.video:description: At least eight people have been killed in an Israeli strike in the southern coastal city of Sidon, Lebanon’s Health Ministry said Friday. Nine others were wounded.

video:video.video:title: Iranian-Americans have mixed feelings as war continues in their homeland
video:video.video:description: Following news of the death of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, many in the Iranian American diaspora in the U.S. celebrated, even as they remain worried for what comes next in their homeland.

video:video.video:title: AI call assistants and connected ambulances on show at MWC
video:video.video:description: Connected hospitals, real-time 3D models and more - the latest technology is going on show at MWC mobile fair in Barcelona.Deutsche Telekom unveiled an AI-powered call assistant, providing live translation, call summaries and answering all kinds of questions.

video:video.video:title: Ryan Gosling: Rocky robot had to meet fan expectations in 'Project Hail Mary'
video:video.video:description: "Project Hail Mary" has a passionate fan following, and strong opinions about the beloved robot character Rocky. (March 9)

video:video.video:title: Oregon Army National Guard hoists injured logger to safety in Mount Hood National Forest
video:video.video:description: An Oregon Army National Guard helicopter safely hoisted an injured logger from a remote national forest on Thursday after steep, rugged terrain prevented other means of rescue, fire department officials said.

video:video.video:title: New attacks target US diplomatic post in northern Iraq
video:video.video:description: Explosions and rising smoke were seen in Irbil on Tuesday, but no casualties were reported after a fresh wave of attacks were intercepted over Irbil, the capital of northern Iraq’s semi-autonomous Kurdish region.

video:video.video:title: Iran war begets fertilizer and other supply cost worries for US farmers
video:video.video:description: Rising fertilizer prices and potential shortages from shipping disruptions due to the Iran war have farmers bracing ahead of the spring planting season.

video:video.video:title: Smoke rises from the Harir base after attacks in the region
video:video.video:description: Smoke rose from the Harir base in northern Iraq on Thursday after attacks targeted the region. Harir air base was used by US and Turkish forces during the international coalitions’ war against the Islamic State militants in Iraq but was evacuated last year.

video:video.video:title: US forecasts blizzard, polar vortex, heat dome and atmospheric river all at once
video:video.video:description: The United States is getting slammed by a stretch of March weather extremes, from flooding rain to record heat and late-season snow.

video:video.video:title: CCTV video shows the moment an Iran missile hit central Israel
video:video.video:description: In the CCTV released by Or Yehuda municipality, a man is seen walking next to a road as a huge explosion occurs.

video:video.video:title: Rescuers dig through rubble of synagogue in central Israel after deadly strike - AP explains
video:video.video:description: First responders dug through rubble in a search for survivors hours after a missile struck a synagogue in a central Israeli town.

video:video.video:title: In Lebanon, displacement and hardship marring run-up to Eid al-Fitr holiday for many
video:video.video:description: As the holy month of Ramadan draws to an end and Muslims worldwide prepare for the typically joyous holiday of Eid al-Fitr, Lebanon has crossed a grim milestone.

video:video.video:title: Sam Claflin starstruck by Ben Whishaw
video:video.video:description: Ever sat opposite a celebrity on the train? Sam Claflin has and - to the delight of his "Vanished" co-star Kaley Cuoco - he describes the moment he spotted "Paddington" star Ben Whishaw.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories March 10
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Tuesday, March 10th: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Tuesday would be the most intense day of strikes in Iran yet; explosive materials found in a Pennsylvania storage unit after homemade bombs thrown in New York; Mississippi Democrats to decide between U.S.

video:video.video:title: Lamborghini launches new $5,300 stroller for parents in the fast lane
video:video.video:description: Does your baby have a need for speed? A new stroller by Italian supercar maker Lamborghini and classic British pram company Silver Cross could be just the luxury transport they need.

video:video.video:title: Drones target oilfields in southern Iraq
video:video.video:description: At least two drones struck energy infrastructure and facilities of U.S. contractors on Friday evening, according to two security officials who spoke on the condition of anonymity as they were not authorized to speak with the press.

video:video.video:title: Macron says US and Israeli strikes on Iran have led to 'an unprecedented regional escalation'
video:video.video:description: French President Emmanuel Macron said Sunday the Iran conflict has prompted France to reinforce its military posture and its defensive support for allies in the Middle East.

video:video.video:title: Iowa Governor honors soldiers killed in a drone strike, says she believes in the mission in Iran
video:video.video:description: Among the six soldiers who died Sunday in Kuwait were two Iowans; Maj. Jeffrey O’Brien, 45, of Indianola, and Sgt. Declan Coady, 20, of West Des Moines.

video:video.video:title: AP reports from the headquarters of Israel’s rescue service
video:video.video:description: With missiles and projectiles launched towards Israel following the U.S.-Israel attacks in Iran, AP reports from the headquarters of Israel’s rescue service to show how the emergency services work.

video:video.video:title: 'Sinners'' Ludwig Göransson on his father and his love for the blues
video:video.video:description: Ludwig Göransson won the Oscar for original score for “Sinners,” winning his third Academy Award. He spoke about his father and his love for the blues.

video:video.video:title: Thousands of Iranian government supporters mourning Khamenei chant 'Death to America'
video:video.video:description: Thousands of Iranian government supporters filled the main square in the central city of Yazd in mourning on Sunday, led by Shiite clerics, after US and Israeli strikes killed Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

video:video.video:title: Seth MacFarlane explains why he used AI in ‘Ted’
video:video.video:description: Seth MacFarlane explains how AI helped him achieve one goal in his TV series "Ted": looking like Bill Clinton.

video:video.video:title: Iran continues unrelenting attacks on shipping, energy infrastructure in Persian Gulf
video:video.video:description: A widening war in Iran has halted oil tankers, made targets of refineries and spooked investors worried about the cascading impact of spiking energy prices.

video:video.video:title: Israeli strikes hit Beirut as conflict widens and intensifies
video:video.video:description: Israeli strikes hit Lebanon's capital Beirut on Monday as the war in the Middle East spiraled further.

video:video.video:title: Mechanical excavator clears rubble after Israeli strikes in Beirut's southern suburbs
video:video.video:description: Excavators cleared debris from a building belonging to Hezbollah’s financial arm, the al-Qard Al-Hasan Association, in Beirut’s southern suburbs on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Trump says war is ‘very complete’ but it’s also ‘the beginning’ of a ‘a new country’
video:video.video:description: The U.S. President was asked whether it was the end or the beginning and said, “It’s the beginning of building a new country,” a comment that seemed to suggest the U.S. might be engaged in the building of a new Iran.

video:video.video:title: Some say the sharks in Las Vegas eat just as well as the tourists
video:video.video:description: The sharks at Mandalay Bay casino are fed to the same standard as the resort's restaurant. With a diet of wild-caught and sustainable fish, some say the sharks eat just as well as the tourists.

video:video.video:title: More than 200 cats compete in Romania's 'Feline Oscars'
video:video.video:description: More than 200 cats from across southeastern Europe competed this weekend at the SofistiCat feline expo in the Romanian capital. (AP video by Nick Dumitrache)

video:video.video:title: US drivers see gas prices jump to their highest level since 2023 as the Iran war drags on
video:video.video:description: Triple-A says the national average for a gallon of regular gasoline jumped to over $3.84 on Wednesday, up from $2.98 consumers were paying before the U.S. and Israel attacked Iran.

video:video.video:title: Buddhists welcome Lunar New Year with relics from remains of enlightened beings
video:video.video:description: The Wei Mountain Temple displays its “10,000 Buddha Relics” every Lunar New Year. Monks bless devotees with shariras – objects culled out from the cremated ashes of Buddhist masters and the Buddha himself – by holding them over people's heads.

video:video.video:title: Indiana man struggles to get a job as labor market remains under strain
video:video.video:description: Since losing his job last November, 25-year-old John Sand said he has applied to nearly 300 jobs, from social media roles to Amazon warehouses, but hasn’t found work yet.

video:video.video:title: Rain brings landslides, rescues, and collapsed roads on Maui
video:video.video:description: Flash flooding has been a major problem in recent days in places like Maui, Molokai and the Big Island, where rain had been falling from 1 to 2 inches (2.5 to 5 centimeters) an hour overnight, according to the Hawaii Emergency Management Agency.

video:video.video:title: Nashville-based reporter for Spanish-language news outlet expected to be freed on bond after ICE detention
video:video.video:description: Estefany Rodríguez Florez' attorneys said that she will likely be able to post bond and be released as soon as tomorrow.

video:video.video:title: Shipping vessels line up as Iran disrupts traffic in Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: Ships were seen lining up in front of Ras Al-Khaimah in the United Arab Emirates on Wednesday as Iran disrupted traffic through the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: First Dubai flight since Iran War started lands at SFO, reuniting stranded travelers with families
video:video.video:description: The first flight from Dubai to San Francisco since the outbreak of the Iran war arrived at SFO, reuniting families and bringing home travelers stranded in the UAE.

video:video.video:title: Debris of an Iranian missile hit the Old City in Jerusalem
video:video.video:description: A strike hit near Jerusalem’s Old City on Friday, close to the hilltop compound home to religious sites revered by Jews and Muslims, marking the danger facing holy sites.

video:video.video:title: 3 Democratic pastors in Iowa are running for Congress, a snapshot of a national trend
video:video.video:description: Amid years of white Christians overwhelmingly supporting Republican President Donald Trump, a striking number of clergy are currently running for political office on the Democratic ticket.

video:video.video:title: Paris Hilton launches recovery fund
video:video.video:description: The pop culture star, advocate and entrepreneur Paris Hilton launched an initiative Monday to support female small-business owners impacted by disasters, a nationwide expansion of her philanthropic support for women entrepreneurs after the 2025 Los Angeles fires.

video:video.video:title: Ready set roll! Conan O'Brien unveils Oscars red carpet
video:video.video:description: Academy Awards host Conan O'Brien joined crew members to roll out the red carpet for Sunday's Oscars at the Dolby Theatre in Los Angeles. (March 11)

video:video.video:title: Veterans react to increasing gas prices, Iran war
video:video.video:description: Bobby Watts, a veteran who served in Operation Desert Storm, said he'd rather see the government spend its tax dollars on helping Americans instead of bombing other countries.

video:video.video:title: Footage released Sea-Watch NGO shows uncrewed Russian tanker drifting in the Mediterranean
video:video.video:description: Footage released on Sunday by the non-governmental organization Sea-Watch showed the uncrewed Russian tanker “Arctic Metagaz” drifting in the Mediterranean.

video:video.video:title: Do you really need a water filter?
video:video.video:description: Experts say most U.S. tap water is fine without a filter. But if you still want one for reassurance, here are some tips.

video:video.video:title: Former President Barack Obama reflects on the life of Rev. Jesse Jackson
video:video.video:description: Former President Barack Obama shares details about his last visit with the late Rev. Jesse Jackson. "He starts coming up with this project and this initiative."

video:video.video:title: Spa day for tortoises: Hungary zoo gives its shell‑ebrity residents the royal treatment
video:video.video:description: Resident tortoises were treated to a comprehensive wellness program by staff at Hungary's Nyíregyháza Zoo.

video:video.video:title: Roy Cooper and Michael Whatley win North Carolina Senate nominations
video:video.video:description: Former Democratic Gov. Roy Cooper and ex-Republican National Committee Chairman Michael Whatley each won their party’s U.S. Senate nominations in North Carolina

video:video.video:title: Stranded traveler in Doha abandons airline wait and drives to Saudi Arabia to reach home
video:video.video:description: An Indian technology entrepreneur stranded in Qatar during the recent Middle East airspace closures has finally made it home after an improvised journey by road and air that took more than two days.

video:video.video:title: John Cornyn and Ken Paxton advance to runoff in Republican primary for Texas US Senate seat
video:video.video:description: Texas Sen. John Cornyn and Attorney General Ken Paxton will compete in a May runoff for the Republican nomination for U.S. Senate.

video:video.video:title: Trump angry with NATO allies over Iran
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump is expressing anger with NATO’s refusal to join his push to send warships to secure the Strait of Hormuz. (AP Video: Mike Pesoli)

video:video.video:title: Police disperse people protesting in support of Iran near Baghdad's Green Zone
video:video.video:description: Police dispersed dozens of Iraqis protesting in support of Iran near Baghdad's Green Zone on Monday night. (AP video: Ali Jabar)

video:video.video:title: Aftermath of airstrike that destroyed a police base in Tehran
video:video.video:description: An Israeli-U.S. airstrike which hit a Tehran police base destroyed the building and heavily damaged its surroundings.

video:video.video:title: 5 indelible AP photos from Hollywood's awards season
video:video.video:description: As a staff photojournalist for The Associated Press, Chris Pizzello covers hundreds of entertainment events a year.

video:video.video:title: Former presidents and former vice president attend Rev. Jesse Jackson's funeral
video:video.video:description: Former Presidents Barack Obama, Bill Clinton and Joe Biden, former Vice President Kamala Harris and former first ladies Jill Biden and Hillary Clinton attended a final public tribute for the late Rev.

video:video.video:title: UK Prime Minister says UK will not join strikes on Iran
video:video.video:description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said on Sunday that the UK will not join in strikes on Iran but has agreed to let the U.S. use British bases for attacks on Iran’s missiles and their launch sites.

video:video.video:title: Foreign ministers from Arab and Muslim countries meet in Riyadh over Iranian attacks on Gulf States
video:video.video:description: A meeting of foreign ministers from Arab and Islamic countries took place in Riyadh on Thursday to discuss regional security amid Iranian attacks on Gulf States.

video:video.video:title: Jeffries applauds firing of DHS chief Kristi Noem, demands policy changes
video:video.video:description: The top Democrat in the U.S House applauded the firing of DHS chief Kristi Noem, while also demanding the Trump administration make policy changes in how Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) operates.

video:video.video:title: 'They are toast and they know it:' Hegseth says Iran war is just beginning
video:video.video:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Wednesday the U.S. and Israel have "only just begun" their war with Iran but declared Tehran "is toast, and they know it."

video:video.video:title: Large part of intercepted Iranian missile removed near homes in northern Israel
video:video.video:description: Members of Shomrey Haaretz, a national volunteer organisation, said the cylindrical shrapnel measured approximately 5.5 meters (18 feet).

video:video.video:title: Warsaw Zoo opens 24-hour drop off point for injured birds
video:video.video:description: Warsaw's accident and emergency room for wounded birds treats around 80 cases a day, all found and brought in by locals. (AP Video: Rafal Niedzielski)

video:video.video:title: Canadian police investigate gunfire at United States consulate in Toronto
video:video.video:description: Royal Canadian Mounted Police Chief Superintendent Chris Leather said the American and Israeli consulates, as well as embassies in Ottawa, will see an increase in security.

video:video.video:title: Hillary Clinton said she never even recalled meeting Jeffrey Epstein
video:video.video:description: Video of former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton answering questions about convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein were released Monday by a House committee investigating the late financier.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he's replacing Homeland Security Secretary Noem with GOP Sen. Markwayne Mullin
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump says he’s picked Oklahoma Sen. Markwayne Mullin to replace Kristi Noem as Secretary of Homeland Security.

video:video.video:title: Pentagon identifies seventh US service member killed in Iran war
video:video.video:description: The Pentagon identified Army Staff Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington, 26, of Glendale, Kentucky, as the seventh U.S. service member killed in the Iran war.

video:video.video:title: US Central Command shows footage of Iranian drone carrier on fire
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military said early Friday that an Iranian drone carrier was attacked and set ablaze. The message from the U.S. military’s Central Command showed black-and-white footage of the carrier ablaze after multiple strikes hit it.

video:video.video:title: Salt-N-Pepa and DJ Spinderella talk Hall of Fame awards
video:video.video:description: Salt-N-Pepa and DJ Spinderella reflect on the importance of their Hall of Fame honors. They were inducted into the Rock-N-Roll Hall of Fame and the NAACP Image Awards Hall of Fame.

video:video.video:title: Ukrainian soldiers commemorate commander Dmytro "Da Vinci" Kotsiubailo during oath-taking ceremony
video:video.video:description: Dmytro Kotsiubailo, callsign Da Vinci, was killed in action three years ago. Soldiers gathered on the grave of Kotsiubailo in Kyiv, singing the national anthem during an oath-taking ceremony for new recruits.

video:video.video:title: Finns go to sauna to break from outside world and find happiness
video:video.video:description: Leave your phone in the locker, get inside a wood-paneled room with your friends, throw some water on heated rocks and relax - this is the receipt of happiness for Finnish people who have topped the list of the happiest countries in the world for the 9th consecutive time.

video:video.video:title: Oldest known whale recording could unlock mysteries of the ocean
video:video.video:description: Researchers say the discovery of the oldest known recordings of whale sounds could open up a new understanding of how the huge animals communicate.

video:video.video:title: Veteran lawmaker was shaped by conflict in the Middle East. Now he's grappling with Iran war
video:video.video:description: As Congress responds to President Trump’s attack on Iran, lawmakers who served in Iraq and Afghanistan find themselves front and center in a war debate that has taken on intensely personal meaning.

video:video.video:title: Casino donates food to unpaid TSA workers in Las Vegas as shutdown continues
video:video.video:description: MGM Resorts brought lunch for TSA workers in Las Vegas as the partial government shutdown continues. (AP Video by Ty ONeil)

video:video.video:title: James Talarico wins Senate Democratic nomination
video:video.video:description: State Rep. James Talarico topped congresswoman Jasmine Crockett in an expensive and fiercely contested Texas Senate Democratic primary that once again has the party dreaming of a big upset in November.

video:video.video:title: Michael B. Jordan on portraying twins in 'Sinners'
video:video.video:description: Journey to the Oscars: At the "Sinners" premiere last year, Michael B. Jordan explained the preparation that went into portraying his twin characters, Smoke and Stack.

video:video.video:title: Supporters in Iran celebrate appointment of Mojtaba Khamenei as new supreme leader
video:video.video:description: Supporters took to streets of Tehran early on Monday to celebrate the appointment of Mojtaba Khamenei as Iran's new supreme leader.

video:video.video:title: Gabbard insists only Trump can determine if Iran poses imminent threat - 'false' says Dem. Senator
video:video.video:description: Democratic senators press DNI Tulsi Gabbard about whether she had advised President Donald Trump that Tehran would retaliate against nearby Gulf nations and block the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: Spain honors rape survivor Gisèle Pelicot with civil award for fight against sexual violence
video:video.video:description: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez awarded French rape survivor Gisèle Pelicot one of country's highest civil awards in recognition of her fight against sexual violence.

video:video.video:title: French woman creates hedgehog rescue centre at home near Paris
video:video.video:description: Sara Stahl created a hedgehog rescue center at her home near Paris, where she has treated more than 1,000 hedgehogs in eight years.

video:video.video:title: Trump says Iran's navy, air force 'is gone,' and offers 'total immunity' to Iranian forces
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Thursday Iran’s navy and air force are “gone” and suggested Tehran’s leaders are ready to make a deal.

video:video.video:title: Trump says chief of staff Susie Wiles has breast cancer but will keep working through treatment
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump says White House chief of staff Susie Wiles has been diagnosed with early stage breast cancer but will continue working during her treatment.

video:video.video:title: Flowers are placed at Chuck Norris' Walk of Fame star
video:video.video:description: Flowers are placed at Chuck Norris’ Walk of Fame star after the actor and martial artist died Thursday at 86. (March 20)

video:video.video:title: How warm weather affects racing at the Winter Paralympics
video:video.video:description: At the Winter Paralympics, some athletes competed in shorts and T-shirts. The warmth is increasingly commonplace in March as temperatures rise with emissions.

video:video.video:title: US Central Command releases video of strikes on Iranian planes, boats, trucks
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military’s Central Command published footage showing what it said was strikes targeting Iranian military aircraft, trucks and boats.

video:video.video:title: It's Shohei Ohtani time at the World Baseball Classic
video:video.video:description: Shohei Ohtani is the show again in Japan for the World Baseball Classic. Japan is the defending champion and is expected to claim one of the two spots for the quarterfinals in the United States.

video:video.video:title: Thousands gather in Istanbul to celebrate Nowruz festival
video:video.video:description: Thousands gathered in Istanbul on Sunday to mark the Nowruz festival. Nowruz, the Farsi word for “new year,” is an ancient Persian festival that is celebrated in Iran and also by Kurds in Turkey, Syria and Iraq.

video:video.video:title: People "bathe" in nature to get respite from chaotic news cycle
video:video.video:description: It might seem impossible to get away from the constant barrage of horrible news these days. But at an urban North Carolina arboretum, people are "bathing" in nature to wash away the stress.

video:video.video:title: Ryan Gosling reveals his 'harshest critics'
video:video.video:description: Ryan Gosling says his daughters inspired one of his wardrobe choices in "Project Hail Mary." He plays a science teacher who journeys to space to save the world in the sci-fi film.

video:video.video:title: How to check US airport security lines while TSA agents go unpaid
video:video.video:description: How to check US airport security lines while TSA agents go unpaid

video:video.video:title: The next generation is watching 'The Pitt'
video:video.video:description: Supriya Ganesh on what she learned about "The Pitt" audience at an Addison Rae concert. The ensemble cast won at the Actor Awards.

video:video.video:title: Trump fires Homeland Security Secretary Noem after mounting criticism
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump on Thursday fired his embattled Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem, after mounting criticism over her leadership of the department, including the handling of the administration’s immigration crackdown and disaster response.

video:video.video:title: How the Iran war could affect your wallet
video:video.video:description: As the price of crude oil surpassed $110 a barrel Monday, reaching heights not seen since 2022, consumers are feeling the effects of the Iran war and its damage to worldwide energy production.

video:video.video:title: 'We are living in a time when it can be hard to hope,' Obama swipes at Trump during Jackson eulogy
video:video.video:description: Former President Barack Obama took a swipe at President Donald Trump, without mentioning him by name, at the final public tribute to the late civil rights leader, the Rev.

video:video.video:title: Protesters stop traffic outside Oscars
video:video.video:description: Police detained at least one protester on Sunday, part of a group blocking traffic near the Oscars. Protesters wearing shirts saying, “Stop child trafficking” huddled in the middle of the road a few blocks from the Oscars.

video:video.video:title: Attack in northern Israel leaves several injured, emergency services say
video:video.video:description: Israel's emergency services, Magen David Adom, said some 30 people had been hurt in a missile attack on Zarzir, about 30 kilometres from Haifa, in northern Israel.

video:video.video:title: Long lines persist at airports as DHS shutdown affects security
video:video.video:description: Travelers are told to arrive the airport hours ahead of their flights on Wednesday at George Bush Intercontinental Airport in Houston as airline passengers wait in long lines to get through the TSA security screening — lasting hours in some cases (AP video: Lekan Oyekanmi)

video:video.video:title: Spring in India: Colourful powder fills the air as an early Holi festival begins
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of people gathered here at a temple believed to be the birthplace of Lord Krishna, one of the most revered Hindu gods.

video:video.video:title: Conan O'Brien not fazed by a celebrity audience
video:video.video:description: Academy Award show host Conan O'Brien says he's not looking out for famous faces in the Oscars audience to see if they like his jokes.

video:video.video:title: Jury deliberating in corruption trial of ex-FirstEnergy executives accused of $4.3 million bribe
video:video.video:description: Jurors in Ohio have begun deliberating in the corruption trial of two fired FirstEnergy Corp. executives charged for their alleged roles in the $60 million bribery scheme centered on a $1 billion nuclear plant bailout.

video:video.video:title: stars show support at Jordan Chiles' final UCLA home meet
video:video.video:description: Keke Palmer, Olandria Carthen, Chloe Bailey, Winnie Harlow, Normani and Lexie Brown show their support at Olympic gymnast Jordan Chiles' final UCLA home meet.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories March 9
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Monday, March 9th: People flee southern Beirut as Israeli attacks continue; UN officials call for stronger access to justice for women; Two men who brought explosives to New York's mayoral mansion held without bail; French woman rescues hedgehogs in Paris

video:video.video:title: Iraqis in Baghdad rally for Mojtaba Khamenei, Iran's next leader
video:video.video:description: Dozens of Iraqis demonstrated in Baghdad on Monday to show support for Mojtaba Khamenei, the son of Iran’s late supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who has been named as the Islamic Republic’s next ruler.

video:video.video:title: Hawaii lei face competition from cheaper imports
video:video.video:description: The flowers in that bright-purple lei you may have received on your Hawaii vacation almost certainly weren't grown in the Aloha State.

video:video.video:title: Multiple gunshots fired near Rihanna’s LA home and a woman is taken into custody
video:video.video:description: Los Angeles police took a woman into custody after she fired gunshots near the home of Grammy-Award winning performer Rihanna, according to police and news reports.

video:video.video:title: Fires engulf vehicles in central Israel after Iranian missile salvos
video:video.video:description: Firefighters extinguished fires in scorched vehicles in central Israel following missile salvos from Iran on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Nepalis prepare for Thursday's first election after protests that ousted the previous government
video:video.video:description: Millions of Nepalis are set to vote Thursday in the first parliamentary elections since a violent youth-led uprising toppled the previous government.

video:video.video:title: British lawmaker and Irish hip-hop trio Kneecap in Cuba as humanitarian convoy arrives on the island
video:video.video:description: British lawmaker and former Labour party leader Jeremy Corbyn called on nations to "stand up" to the U.S. over its continued blockade of Cuba.

video:video.video:title: Celebrity chef René Redzepi resigns from iconic Danish restaurant Noma after abuse allegations
video:video.video:description: René Redzepi, founder and celebrity chef at the iconic Danish restaurant Noma, has stepped down from his post following allegations of abuse and assault at the Copenhagen landmark.

video:video.video:title: Three Palestinians killed in West Bank in Iranian missile attack - AP dispatch
video:video.video:description: The Palestinian Health Ministry says three women were killed and three others, including a child, were wounded in the overnight attack.

video:video.video:title: Watch: People flee Beirut's southern suburbs after Israeli strikes hit the area
video:video.video:description: Scores of people were fleeing Beirut's southern suburbs early Monday after Israel launched a series of strikes in retaliation for missiles fired across the border by the Lebanese militant group Hezbollah.

video:video.video:title: Travelers caught in the Mideast conflict react as they finally return home
video:video.video:description: Frustrated and anxious travelers clamored Tuesday for flights out of the Middle East and other regions where a widening Iran war has stranded tens of thousands of people, closed major airports and caused widespread cancellations.

video:video.video:title: Hegseth says 'we hold the cards' as he warns Iran against further attacks on neighbors
video:video.video:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth says the U.S. military “controls the fate” of Iran. He said that Tehran “should not, going forward, target Arab allies, Arab countries, trying to create pain, the pain that they created themselves.”

video:video.video:title: Jessie Buckley, Timothée Chalamet pose at Oscars
video:video.video:description: Stars including Jessie Buckley, Timothée Chalamet and Leonardo DiCaprio pose for photographs ahead of the 98th Academy Awards. (March 15)

video:video.video:title: Pakistan struggles to mitigate the fuel crisis in wake of Iran-US and Israel conflict
video:video.video:description: The war with Iran is doing collateral damage to the world economy. Pakistan finds itself in an especially bleak position.

video:video.video:title: Texan James Talarico becomes a fresh face of Democrats' midterm hopes after Senate primary win
video:video.video:description: James Talarico barely mentioned Donald Trump when he finally got to give his victory speech on Wednesday night as Democrats' nominee for U.S. Senate in Texas.

video:video.video:title: House narrowly rejects Iran war powers resolution in early test of Trump's strategy
video:video.video:description: The House narrowly rejected a war powers resolution that would halt President Donald Trump’s attack on Iran. The Senate defeated a similar measure on Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Israel says a man was killed in an Iranian missile attack - AP explains
video:video.video:description: The man was killed in an attack that targeted the city of Yehud, in central Israel. This marked the first death from missiles in Israel in a week, as AP's Sam Mednick reports from the scene.

video:video.video:title: Video shows huge blast near US consulate in Dubai
video:video.video:description: An Iranian drone slammed into a parking lot outside the U.S. consulate in Dubai on Tuesday, sparking a small fire, according to U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio.

video:video.video:title: Tehran buildings reduced to rubble after Israeli-US strikes
video:video.video:description: During a media tour organized by Iran's government, machinery was seen working on Monday to remove the rubble of buildings damaged by Israeli-U.S. strikes overnight in Tehran.

video:video.video:title: Dust storm blows over Gaza as people are urged to protect themselves

video:video.video:description: Visitors are rushing to Death Valley National Park to take in a rare phenomenon known as a superbloom. (AP Video by Ty ONeil)

video:video.video:description: U.S. Coast Guard rescues a person stranded on ice near Mackinac Island, Michigan.

video:video.video:title: Fed holds interest rates steady: here’s what that means for consumers

video:video.video:description: The S&P 500 fell as much as 1.2% Monday and finished with a gain of less than 0.1%. The Dow added 0.1%, and the Nasdaq composite rose 0.4%.

video:video.video:description: As U.S. and Israeli forces pounded Iran, and Tehran and its affiliates retaliated by firing missiles at targets across the Mideast on Monday, the international legal order was caught in the crossfire.

video:video.video:description: Sirens sounded again in Tel Aviv, Israel on Friday following launches from Iran. Trails from interceptions were visible in the sky, and the sound of explosions could be heard

video:video.video:description: A hotel parking lot in Dubai was converted into a shelter for hotel guests seeking safety amid incoming strikes from Iran, which has hit the UAE and several of its neighbors in retaliation for the major attack by U.S. and Israeli forces.

video:video.video:description: Venezuelan opposition leader María Corina Machado on Thursday said millions who left their country can return and help rebuild it. (AP video shot by Mauricio Cuevas)
