video:video.video:description: Soldiers were deployed to the streets of South Africa’s biggest city Wednesday in an effort to help police fight gang violence and illegal mining.

video:video.video:description: For the first time, more than 20 casts of those who died in the catastrophic volcano eruption in Pompeii in 79 AD, are going on display together in a new, permanent exhibition.

video:video.video:description: Oil prices have surged as the Iran war threatens production and shipping in the Middle East. Here’s how the conflict is fueling fears of a wider global energy crisis.

video:video.video:description: The six members of the Army Reserve, who worked in logistics and kept troops supplied with food and equipment, died when a drone hit a command center in Port Shuaiba, Kuwait, one day after the U.S. and Israel launched their military campaign against Iran.

video:video.video:description: The United Nations estimates the entire enclave has more than 61 million tons of debris. Meanwhile Palestinians continue to live in tents amid the rubble, without access to infrastructure or facilities.

video:video.video:description: Documents released by the British government show officials believed there was a “reputational risk” to appointing Peter Mandelson as the U.S. ambassador because of his relationship with convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

video:video.video:description: Retired Lebanese security officer Captain Ahmed Shukr vanished in December after going to meet a would-be buyer of a plot of land he was trying to sell.

video:video.video:description: A Hezbollah official said Thursday that a building in the Zoqaq el-Blat neighborhood of Beirut that Israel alleged was a branch of Hezbollah's financial arm and was damaged in Israeli airstrikes had been closed for "two weeks."

video:video.video:description: Khamenei was killed in a major attack on Iran launched by Israel and the United States, throwing the future of the Islamic Republic into doubt and raising the risk of regional instability.

video:video.video:title: Moroccans turn to gold jewellery as a safer investment

video:video.video:description: Tim Walz and Keith Ellison were called to testify before the panel exploring fraud and misuse of government funds in the state.

video:video.video:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is visiting MacDill Air Force Base in Florida and held meetings with Adm. Brad Cooper, who leads U.S. Central Command.

video:video.video:description: Three American fighter jets were mistakenly downed by friendly Kuwaiti fire during an Iranian air assault, the U.S. military said.

video:video.video:description: Production of mezcal in Mexico is booming to meet growing global demand. The boom has both created opportunities for producers and come with environmental costs.

video:video.video:description: While strikes continue hitting the capital of Iran, dozens of people gathered in Tehran on Monday to bury those killed by Israeli-US strikes in the last few days.

video:video.video:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth wouldn't rule out U.S. troops on the ground in Iran, but declared the United States would not be dragged into an “endless” war.

video:video.video:description: It's St. Patrick's day, and visitors to Ireland's rural coastline can find a number of unusual places to stay.

video:video.video:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth dismisses questions about the time frame of the widening conflict in Iran, saying: “President Trump has all the latitude in the world to talk about how long it may or may not take.

video:video.video:description: Iraq is getting caught in the crossfire of the Iran war as the only country facing strikes from both sides, and that threatens to drag the nation that has so far avoided two years of regional turmoil into a full-blown crisis.

video:video.video:description: Israeli tanks were seen being transferred to the border with Lebanon on Thursday as fighting between Israel and Hezbollah escalated amid the wider Iran war.

video:video.video:description: People demonstrated in New York on Sunday in support of U.S. strikes in Iran and leadership change in the country.

video:video.video:description: Penn objects to federal judge demands for lists revealing workers’ Jewish faith, ancestry or ties to Jewish organizations. (AP Video: Tassanee Vejpongsa)

video:video.video:description: Police are investigating whether a bus fire in a town west of the Swiss capital that killed at least six people was started deliberately.

video:video.video:title: YouTuber Jake Paul joins Trump in Kentucky midterm rally
video:video.video:description: Youtuber Jake Paul also joined Trump onstage as the president visited Rep. Thomas Massie's Kentucky district hoping to see him defeated in an upcoming GOP primary.

video:video.video:title: Explosions rock Beirut as plumes of smoke fill the sky
video:video.video:description: Large explosions and plumes of smoke were seen in Beirut on Tuesday. Earlier in the day, Lebanon's army said five of its soldiers were hurt after a morning airstrike on the village of Kfar Sir, two of them being seriously wounded.

video:video.video:description: A giant steel pipe protruded from the ground on Wednesday on a highway in Osaka city in western Japan, rising as high as 13 metres above ground, according to local authorities.

video:video.video:description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom on Wednesday said he’s still “processing” the stunning abuse allegations against César Chavez, the long-admired Latino icon, and urged for more reflection.

video:video.video:description: Muddy floodwaters from severe rains have inundated streets, swallowed vehicles and prompted evacuation orders for more than 4,000 people in towns north of Honolulu.

video:video.video:title: Hong Kong investors remain cautious amid escalation in the Middle East
video:video.video:description: Asian shares have swooned, with South Korea's benchmark plunging 10%, after a global sell-off for stocks hit Wall Street. Oil prices climbed still higher.

video:video.video:description: Hundreds gathered in the northern Indian city of Mathura at a temple believed to be the birthplace of Lord Krishna, one of the most revered Hindu gods.

video:video.video:title: 'KPop Demon Hunters' star "thrilled" for sequel
video:video.video:description: "KPop Demon Hunters" star Arden Cho hints that the newly-announced sequel to the Oscar-nominated animated feature may arrive sooner than we think.

video:video.video:title: Beautiful hidden cloister once a centre of Rome's religious intrigue
video:video.video:description: In the heart of Rome, just a stone’s throw from the Pantheon, there is a fascinating place that is normally closed to the public: the marvelous Guidetti Cloister of the Basilica of Santa Maria sopra Minerva.

video:video.video:description: Hundreds of people released lanterns into Taiwan’s night sky at an annual festival in New Taipei on Tuesday evening. (AP video shot by: Taijing Wu)

video:video.video:title: Ukraine firefighters battle intense blaze after deadly Russian drone strike on Zaporizhzhia
video:video.video:description: Ukrainian firefighters battled an intense blaze after a deadly Russian drone strike on Zaporizhzhia early Monday.

video:video.video:title: Oscars host Conan O'Brien helps roll out the red carpet
video:video.video:description: Academy Awards host Conan O'Brien joined crew members to roll out the red carpet for Sunday's Oscars at the Dolby Theatre in Los Angeles.

video:video.video:title: Oscar-nominated Tunisian filmmaker: 'Cinema can change things'
video:video.video:description: Kaouther Ben Hania says "cinema can change things" as U.S. lawmakers back legislation calling for an investigation into the killing of the Palestinian girl at the heart of her Oscar-nominated film, "The Voice of Hind Rajab."

video:video.video:title: Tourists’ sweat creates a salty film coating Michelangelo’s ’The Last Judgment’
video:video.video:description: Michelangelo’s “The Last Judgement” is getting a facial, with restorers removing a chalky white film of salt that has accumulated over the Renaissance masterpiece since its last major renovation three decades ago.

video:video.video:description: A remnant of Florida's failed "Panama Canal" from the 1960's -- the Rodman Kirkpatrick Dam-- is blocking manatee and fish migrations and should be removed, environmentalists say.

video:video.video:description: The Winter Paralympics are in Milan Cortina to celebrate their 50th anniversary, with China looking to extend its dominance as a Paralympic powerhouse and Ukraine and other nations boycotting the opening ceremony over the return of the Russian flag and anthem.

video:video.video:title: Israel PM orders coffee and dispels rumors of his death in online post
video:video.video:description: The video was posted on Benjamin Netanyahu's Facebook page, responding to viral posts claiming he'd been killed, which were followed by posts speculating that footage of him had been AI edited because he looked to have six fingers.

video:video.video:title: Chile's new far-right president launches work on border barrier
video:video.video:description: Chilean President José Antonio Kast on Monday began overseeing preparations to build a border barrier — part of his flagship campaign promise to block immigrants from crossing illegally.

video:video.video:title: Israeli warplanes bomb Tehran
video:video.video:description: Israeli warplanes bombed Tehran early on Friday and again on Saturday. Meanwhile, U.S. President Donald Trump appeared to rule out talks with Iran in a social media post calling for its “unconditional surrender.”

video:video.video:title: As heat rises, so do complaints about stuffy subway rides
video:video.video:description: As temperatures rise above ground, the number of subway riders reporting uncomfortable heat belowground increases, according to a new study in the journal Nature Cities on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Vote count begins after Colombians cast ballot for Congress and presidential candidates
video:video.video:description: Colombians voted Sunday for a new Congress and to select candidates from three major coalitions in a primary-style contest ahead of a presidential election in May.

video:video.video:title: Refusing to answer questions on Iran school strike, Hegseth says officer picked to lead investigation
video:video.video:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth says U.S. Central Command has picked a lead officer to investigate a deadly strike on a school in Iran.

video:video.video:title: Starmer warned of ‘reputational risk’ in appointing Mandelson but ignored red flags, files show
video:video.video:description: Starmer fired Mandelson after nine months in the job when new details of the relationship with Epstein emerged, and now faces a political storm over the appointment.

video:video.video:title: BLACKPINK's Jisoo teases solo album
video:video.video:description: BLACKPINK’s Jisoo says the band’s collaboration with Coldplay’s Chris Martin brought “fresh” energy to their latest album.

video:video.video:title: Russia launches Progress MS-33 cargo spacecraft to International Space Station
video:video.video:description: A Soyuz-2.1a launch vehicle carrying the Progress MS-33 cargo spacecraft was launched from the Baikonur Cosmodrome in Kazakhstan.

video:video.video:title: Israeli warplanes bomb Tehran as war in the region escalates
video:video.video:description: Israeli warplanes bombed Tehran early on Friday while U.S. President appeared to rule out talks with Iran in a social media post calling for its “unconditional surrender.”

video:video.video:title: Hegseth warns Tuesday will be the 'most intense day of strikes inside Iran'
video:video.video:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth warns that Tuesday “will be yet again our most intense day of strikes inside Iran: The most fighters, the most bombers, the most strikes, intelligence more refined and better than ever.”

video:video.video:title: Israelis living near border face renewed Israel–Hezbollah clashes
video:video.video:description: Lebanon was dragged into the war in the Middle East early Monday, which erupted following U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran.

video:video.video:title: ‘The Secret Agent’ director on Oscars in wartime
video:video.video:description: "The Secret Agent" writer-director Kleber Mendonça Filho says it's a balancing act for filmmakers to celebrate at the Oscars during a war in the Middle East. (March 13)

video:video.video:title: Kim Jong Un sits on a tank with his daughter at a military exercise
video:video.video:description: In their latest joint appearance, North Korean leader Kim Jong Un and his teenage daughter rode a tank together, state media video showed Friday, days after they watched rocket launches and fired pistols.

video:video.video:title: Kew Gardens in London bursts into bloom as spring starts to arrive
video:video.video:description: After a miserable, wet winter, spring has sprung in London, with the city’s Kew Gardens in full bloom. Signs of new life are abundant at this UNESCO World Heritage Site.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories March 19
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Thursday, March 19th: Pentagon seeking additional $200 billion for Iran war; California lawmakers want to rename César Chavez Day; Son of Norway’s crown princess faces prison sentence; record-breaking heat across Southwest to continue into the weekend.

video:video.video:title: Orbán and challenger Magyar summon rival rallies in show of strength before Hungary's election
video:video.video:description: Tens of thousands of supporters gathered in Budapest on Sunday in rival rallies to show their backing for Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán and his challenger Péter Magyar in the upcoming elections.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories March 12
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Thursday, March 12th: Iran strikes Bahrain’s International Airport; U.S. releases more video of strikes in Iran; Trump comments on war; Cambodia shuts down 200 scam centers.

video:video.video:title: Former ’19 Kids and Counting’ star Joseph Duggar arrested on child molestation charge
video:video.video:description: Joseph Duggar, 31, is charged with lewd and lascivious behavior on a child under 12 years old, according to an arrest affidavit from the Bay County Sheriff’s Office in Panama City, Florida.

video:video.video:title: Iranians celebrate after Mojtaba Khamenei named supreme leader
video:video.video:description: Mojtaba Khamenei, the son of Iran's late supreme leader, has been named his successor, Iranian state TV announced early Monday, as the war that began with his father's killing less than two weeks ago took a dramatic turn.

video:video.video:title: Staff struggle to restrain dancing robot
video:video.video:description: Staff at a Cupertino, California, restaurant restrained a dancing robot after it knocked items off a table, sending chopsticks and sauce flying, video shows.

video:video.video:title: Gunman was not on the FBI's radar before he opened fire on a crowded Texas bar, authorities say
video:video.video:description: The man who opened fire outside a crowded Texas bar and killed two people in an attack that wounded 14 others was not on the radar of authorities before the shooting, federal and local investigators said Monday.

video:video.video:title: Star of 'KPop Demon Hunters' proud of Korean American roots
video:video.video:description: EJAE, who provides the singing voice of Rumi in “KPop Demon Hunters,” said she was “proud” to return to her roots as a Korean American woman during her Academy Awards performance of the best original song winner “Golden.”

video:video.video:title: 2 Ohio pastors emerge as faithful allies for Haitian migrants during Trump's crackdown
video:video.video:description: One grew up in rural Haiti amid the poverty and violence. The other grew up in Michigan as a self-described “blue-collar farm kid."

video:video.video:title: Rapper Afroman wins lawsuit against police over mocking their 2022 raid in viral music videos
video:video.video:description: The Grammy-nominated rapper Afroman has won a defamation lawsuit filed by seven Ohio sheriff’s deputies who sued him over music videos that used home security footage to mock them over a 2022 raid of his home.

video:video.video:title: Hong Kong and Japan markets react to attack on Iran
video:video.video:description: Asia markets in Hong Kong and Japan react after the U.S. and Israel's attacks on Iran.

video:video.video:title: Police fire tear gas during protest against labor reform in Ecuador
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of workers, students, and teachers protested Friday in Ecuador’s capital Quito after the government extended daily work shift from eight to 10 hours, with a weekly cap of 40 hours.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories March 9
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Monday, March 9th: Iran names new leader: Oil prices soar during Iran conflict; Israeli targets Iran-backed militants in southern Lebanon; U.S. strikes suspected drug boat.

video:video.video:title: A gay Muslim influencer in Germany calls for tolerance during Ramadan
video:video.video:description: Ali Darwich, a gay Muslim influencer, picks up a date from his plate, takes a sip of water, and addresses the 15 friends sitting around the table and breaking the Ramadan fast with him.

video:video.video:title: J Balvin jumps at chance to record FIFA World Cup Anthem
video:video.video:description: J Balvin shares why it was important for him to be part of "Jump," Coca-Cola’s anthem for the FIFA World Cup 2026.

video:video.video:title: Tornadoes cause significant damage in parts of Illinois and Indiana
video:video.video:description: Major storms that whipped up tornadoes in parts of Illinois and Indiana on Tuesday leveled homes, downed trees and power lines, and overwhelmed a 911 center south of Chicago with emergency calls, according to officials.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories March 6
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Friday, March 6: More strikes in Iran; Iran fires missiles and drones at Persian Gulf nations; Americans stranded in Middle East begin returning to U.S.; Trump fires Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem.

video:video.video:description: The U.S. is temporarily easing some sanctions on Russian oil shipments, reflecting global concerns over sharply higher crude prices due to supply shortages stemming from the Iran war.

video:video.video:title: Nightclub bombing in Peru injures 33, including minors, authorities say
video:video.video:description: A bombing at a nightclub in Peru has injured 33 people, including minors, authorities said Saturday.

video:video.video:title: New exhibit spotlights Japanese-American soldiers who fought in World War II
video:video.video:description: A new traveling exhibit tells the story of Japanese American soldiers who fought in World War II, even as their families were incarcerated as enemies of the state.

video:video.video:title: Israel reinforces northern border with Lebanon but has yet to evacuate villages
video:video.video:description: Israeli forces reinforce northern border communities as missiles and drones launch from Lebanon, AP’s Sam Mednick reports.

video:video.video:title: Jack Quaid on 'The Boys' final season: 'No character is safe'
video:video.video:description: "The Boys" stars, including Jack Quaid, Antony Starr and Karl Urban, talked about the surprises in the final season of Prime Video’s superhero satire series, at the show's world premiere in Rome.

video:video.video:title: 'Buffy' star's message to fans over reboot cancellation
video:video.video:description: At a special event in London for her latest film "Ready or Not: Here I Come," Sarah Michelle Gellar says she's been moved by fans' reaction to the cancellation of a "Buffy the Vampire Slayer" reboot.

video:video.video:title: Thousands march in countries across Latin America to mark International Women’s Day
video:video.video:description: Women across Latin America called for equal pay, reproductive rights, justice and celebrated women empowerment during events and demonstrations marking International Women’s Day on Sunday.

video:video.video:description: Video from the U.S. Central Command showed U.S. forces destroying targets on the ground, which it claims were in Kharg Island, Iran.

video:video.video:title: More Israeli-US strikes rock the Iranian capital
video:video.video:description: Damage and debris were scattered near Tehran's Resalat Square following more strikes on the Iranian capital on Tuesday.

video:video.video:description: Russia has fired tens of thousands of Iranian-designed Shahed drones at Ukraine since it invaded its neighbor just over four years ago.

video:video.video:description: “The Secretary-General continues to be very concerned about the situation in Lebanon where the Lebanese people are once again in the crosshairs of conflict," the spokesperson for the Secretary told the press.

video:video.video:description: Vice President JD Vance joined the grieving family of a Kentucky man who was the seventh U.S. service member to die in combat during the Iran war as his remains were brought back to the U.S.

video:video.video:description: Noem’s departure caps a tumultuous tenure overseeing immigration enforcement tactics that have been met with protests and lawsuits, as well as a wave of criticism over the response to deadly Texas floods.

video:video.video:description: Fire crews were working to contain a blaze Wednesday at the fuel storage tanks at Oman's Port of Salalah, amid days of Iranian attacks, according to the Oman News Agency.

video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump awarded the Medal of Honor to three U.S. Army soldiers at the White House on Monday, celebrating heroes of old wars as he defended his launch of a new one.

video:video.video:title: Statues of Founding Fathers added to Trump's revamped Rose Garden
video:video.video:description: Life-size figures of two U.S. Founding Fathers have been added to President Donald Trump's revamped Rose Garden at the White House as the country celebrates its 250th anniversary this year.

video:video.video:title: Displaced Palestinians in Gaza return to a tent camp that was struck by Israel the day before
video:video.video:description: Displaced Palestinians sheltering west of Gaza City returned Thursday morning to a tent camp struck by Israel the day prior.

video:video.video:title: Hundreds killed in Pakistan strike on Afghanistan hospital treating drug users
video:video.video:description: Afghanistan’s deputy government spokesman said early Tuesday the death toll from an airstrike by Pakistan that hit a hospital treating drug users in the Afghan capital Kabul has increased to 400.

video:video.video:description: A giant underground pipe rose more than 32 feet out of a construction site in a busy area of the Japanese city of Osaka overnight, nearly reaching an elevated road without any witnesses.

video:video.video:title: British Prime Minister was warned of 'reputational risk' in Mandelson appointment
video:video.video:description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer was warned that Peter Mandelson ’s friendship with convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein exposed the government to “reputational risk,” but he still appointed him as ambassador to the United States, documents released Wednesday show.

video:video.video:title: Wildflowers are blooming in the driest place in North America — but not for long
video:video.video:description: Video Journalist Ty ONeil goes to Death Valley National Park in California to take in a rare phenomenon known as a superbloom. (Video by: Ty ONeil)

video:video.video:title: What is meningitis B and why is there an outbreak in Kent, UK?
video:video.video:description: Two young people have died: a school pupil in a nearby town and a student at the University of Kent, in Canterbury - 60 miles south-east of London.

video:video.video:title: Bessent insists that if Trump were to delay China trip it would not be due to Strait of Hormuz pressure
video:video.video:description: The US Treasury Secretary said any delay to Trump’s month-end trip to Beijing would not be because of disagreements over the Iran war or efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: Building damaged in Tel Aviv after strike
video:video.video:description: A building was damaged in Tel Aviv following a strike early Thursday. Footage captured by The Associated Press showed emergency services at the scene.

video:video.video:description: Professor Martin Davies, Director of Tulane University's Maritime Law Center says import prices will likely continue to rise for the U.S. consumer as the war goes on.

video:video.video:title: A Lebanese theater offers displaced youth a stage to share war stories as therapy
video:video.video:description: The Lebanese National Theatre has opened its stage to displaced young people, allowing them to transform their experiences of war into performance.

video:video.video:title: UN chief warns that the war in the Middle East 'could spiral beyond anyone's control'
video:video.video:description: U.N. Secretary-General António Guterres called for an end to the war in the Middle East that now affects more than a dozen countries, and for serious diplomatic negotiations, warning the conflict could spiral out of control.

video:video.video:title: Iranian drone hits Dubai tower as attacks reported across Gulf states
video:video.video:description: Firefighters extinguished a blaze in two units at the Address Creek 2 Tower. The high-rise building showed some damage to its upper floors.

video:video.video:title: Clown teaches parents tips to help kids cope with war trauma
video:video.video:description: Sabine Choucair, a clown with 20 years of experience performing for children in displaced refugee camps and disaster areas around the world, is now offering her expertise to parents, teaching them how to help children cope with war trauma.

video:video.video:title: Migrants are vanishing in the Mediterranean, so is access to information from authorities
video:video.video:description: Migrants are vanishing in the Mediterranean, so is access to information from authorities

video:video.video:title: Iranian woman in Indiana views US, Israel attacks as help but says Iranians must determine country’s future
video:video.video:description: Afagh Farhadi, a postdoctoral researcher from Iran who has been in the U.S. since 2017, says she is hopeful about the future of her home country but wants Iranians to control their own destiny.

video:video.video:description: Iran’s place at the men’s World Cup in three months’ time was put in doubt Monday amid an escalating Middle East conflict sparked by the soccer tournament’s co-host the United States.

video:video.video:title: Hegseth says an American submarine sank an Iranian warship with a torpedo in international waters
video:video.video:description: The U.S. sank an Iranian warship in international waters, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Wednesday, as it intensified its bombardment with Israel of Iran's security forces and other symbols of power.

video:video.video:title: Greenpeace stages "No to war" protest in Madrid
video:video.video:description: Protesters opposed to the Iran war climbed onto one of the buildings in the famous Puerta del Sol square in the Spanish capital on Tuesday morning to display a large banner reading “No to war” as commuters were passing by on their way to work.

video:video.video:title: Zohran Mamdani condemns extremism after explosive devices found near the Mayor's home
video:video.video:description: New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani says ‘extremism of any kind will not be tolerated’ as officials investigate what they call ISIS-inspired terrorism after two unexploded devices were found near his residence during a protest.

video:video.video:title: Meteorite hunters in Ohio search for fragments that fell to Earth with a boom

video:video.video:title: Utah woman who wrote a book on grief after husband's death found guilty of murdering him
video:video.video:description: A Utah woman was convicted Monday of aggravated murder after poisoning her husband with fentanyl and then self-publishing a children’s book about coping with grief.

video:video.video:title: Neighbor of man who rammed into Michigan synagogue reacts to attack
video:video.video:description: A neighbor of Ayman Mohamad Ghazali, who was killed by security after ramming a vehicle into Temple Israel in West Bloomfield Township near Detroit on Thursday, reacts to attack.

video:video.video:title: A chance to own Kurt Cobain's 'Smells Like Teen Spirit' guitar or John Lennon's piano
video:video.video:description: Christie's auction house in New York is set to have one of the most extensive Rock n Roll and pop culture auctions in recent history next week.

video:video.video:title: Conan O'Brien on keeping Oscars jokes timely
video:video.video:description: Oscars host Conan O’Brien says the speed of the news cycle means jokes written a month ago are already 'Eh.' (Mar. 16)

video:video.video:title: César Chavez accused of sexually abusing labor rights leader Dolores Huerta, AP Explains
video:video.video:description: Labor rights activist Dolores Huerta revealed she was among women and girls who say they were sexually abused by César Chavez, the long-admired Latino icon, while he led the United Farm Workers union.

video:video.video:title: Scream clubs: A vocal release for stress
video:video.video:description: Can you scream your way to happiness? Maybe not, but the scream clubs popping up around the U.S. promise some relief. (AP Video: Emilie Megnien, Laura Bargfeld)

video:video.video:title: For survivors, Rhode Island clergy abuse report brings vindication
video:video.video:description: A report by Rhode Island’s attorney general detailing decades of sexual abuse by Catholic clergy is giving survivors a sense of vindication while renewing calls for accountability and support.

video:video.video:title: Beyond the Potomac River, sewage spills threaten cities with old infrastructure and little funds
video:video.video:description: A major sewer pipe collapse has dumped hundreds of millions of gallons of sewage into the Potomac River, highlighting a nationwide problem involving failing infrastructure.

video:video.video:title: Protesters block bus believed to be carrying Iran women's soccer team in Australia
video:video.video:description: Australia granted asylum to five members of the Iranian women’s soccer team who were visiting the country for a tournament when the Iran war began, a government minister said Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Georgia voters choosing a successor to Marjorie Taylor Greene in US House
video:video.video:description: Months after Marjorie Taylor Greene resigned from Congress following a falling-out with President Donald Trump, voters in her Georgia district headed to the polls Tuesday to choose her successor in a special election.

video:video.video:title: Residents of Cuba capital struggle as islandwide blackout hits in deepening energy crisis
video:video.video:description: Officials in Cuba reported an island-wide blackout Monday in the country of some 11 million people as its energy and economic crises deepen and its power grid continues to crumble.

video:video.video:title: Funeral held in Tehran for Iran's intelligence minister and his family
video:video.video:description: A funeral was held in Tehran for Iran's slain intelligence minister and his family on Friday.

video:video.video:title: Endangered monkey gives birth after pioneering surgery
video:video.video:description: A critically endangered roloway monkey has given birth at Chester Zoo, months after a complex operation saved her foot from amputation.

video:video.video:title: J Balvin jumps at chance to record FIFA World Cup anthem
video:video.video:description: J Balvin helped reimagine Van Halen’s "Jump" for Coca-Cola’s official anthem for the FIFA World Cup 2026.

video:video.video:title: Trump hosts White House roundtable aimed at 'saving college sports'
video:video.video:description: Last year, President Donald Trump endorsed the the Score Act, which would set new rules for the payment of college athletes and set nationwide standards for compensation, but the act has stalled in Congress.

video:video.video:title: Top-secret Cold War nuclear bunker becomes a popular tourist attraction in Albania
video:video.video:description: A spartan nuclear bunker built during the Cold War rule of the late communist dictator Enver Hoxha could have become a forgotten relic of his communist regime, instead it's become a tourist hotspot.

video:video.video:title: Sri Lanka recovers 87 bodies from Iranian warship sunk off its coast by a US submarine
video:video.video:description: A torpedo fired by a U.S. submarine sank an Iranian warship off the coast of Sri Lanka, whose navy said Wednesday it recovered 87 bodies and rescued 32 people.

video:video.video:title: Dozens injured in Israel after Iranian missile strikes target areas near nuclear research center
video:video.video:description: Iran struck two communities near Israel’s main nuclear research center late Saturday, leaving buildings shattered and at least seven people seriously injured, hours after Tehran's main nuclear enrichment site was hit as the war spun into a dangerous new direction at the start of its fourth week.

video:video.video:title: Footage captures a powerful tornado in Michigan devastating homes in its path
video:video.video:description: At Lisa Piper's home near Union City, Michigan, she can be heard repeatedly yelling out, as she films from her back deck a ferocious rotating column of air that appears to be a tornado tear through a section of buildings across the lake from her.

video:video.video:title: Zelenskyy claims Ukraine's military 'thwarted' Russian offensive planned for March
video:video.video:description: Zelenskyy claims Ukraine's military 'thwarted' Russian offensive planned for March

video:video.video:title: 'Outlander' cast says goodbye in New York
video:video.video:description: After more than a decade of time travel, war, romance and revolution, the stars of “Outlander” launch the show's eighth and final season at a New York premiere.

video:video.video:title: New photos of Neil Armstrong from the Gemini 8 mission released
video:video.video:description: Newly released photographs are shedding light on a little known but critical moment in Neil Armstrong’s career, years before he became the first person to walk on the moon.

video:video.video:title: Strong rains flood homes in Colombia
video:video.video:description: Emergency workers on Friday were assisting residents in Facatativá, on the outskirts of the Colombian capital, who saw their homes and town flooded following strong rains in recent days.

video:video.video:title: Liberia's largest gold miner spilled dangerous chemicals, records show
video:video.video:description: For generations, families in Jikandor village fished and drank water from a river that runs through Liberia’s dense rainforest.

video:video.video:title: Iran hosts public funeral for commanders killed on the first day of the war
video:video.video:description: Mourners and supporters of the Iranian government attended a funeral in Tehran on Wednesday for senior military commanders and others killed during the ongoing war with the United States and Israel.

video:video.video:title: Jobs report reveals unexpected 92,000 job cuts as unemployment rises
video:video.video:description: U.S. employers cut 92,000 jobs last month and pushed unemployment up to 4.4% in what economist Diane Swonk calls a “jobless boom.” (AP Video: Ted Shaffrey)

video:video.video:title: Iran war raises worries in the US over higher gas prices
video:video.video:description: Oil surged higher and showed no signs of halting its rapid climb a week after the U.S. and Israel launched major attacks on Iran that escalated into a war in the Middle East.

video:video.video:title: 'Suspicious device' found in Manhattan, day after explosive device thrown at anti-Islam protest
video:video.video:description: Police in New York City on Sunday said that they were looking into a second suspicious device found in the same area of Manhattan's Upper East Side where a counterprotester during an anti-Islam demonstration threw an improvised explosive.

video:video.video:title: Viola Davis talks Black maternal health in 'The Ebony Canal'
video:video.video:description: Viola Davis talks about why she's supporting "The Ebony Canal," a documentary film she narrated that examines Black maternal health, and disparities in infant mortality.

video:video.video:title: Island-wide blackout hits Cuba as island struggles with deepening energy crisis
video:video.video:description: Officials in Cuba reported an island-wide blackout Monday in the country of some 11 million people as its energy and economic crises deepen and its power grid continues to crumble.

video:video.video:title: Multiple gunshots fired near Rihanna’s LA home
video:video.video:description: Los Angeles police took a woman into custody after she fired gunshots near Rihanna's home, according to police and news reports.

video:video.video:title: Raves rise in Lagos as young Nigerians seek alternative party scene
video:video.video:description: For decades Nigerian clubs revolved around “table culture”— a system separating high-spending guests from others and reinforcing social status.

video:video.video:title: An inside look at an AP reporter's missile bunker in Jerusalem
video:video.video:description: AP reporter Julia Frankel saw people running toward shelters in Jerusalem while sirens blared out announcing an incoming attack on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Iran confirms death of 86-year-old Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei
video:video.video:description: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who assembled theocratic power in Iran over the decades as its supreme leader and sought to turn it into a regional powerhouse, bringing it into confrontation with Israel and the United States over its nuclear program while crushing democracy protesters at home, has died.

video:video.video:title: Gulf states call on UN Security Council to condemn 'Iran's unprovoked aggression' ahead of vote
video:video.video:description: The Council will vote on a draft resolution submitted by Bahrain on behalf of the Gulf Cooperation Council.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories March 2
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Monday, March 2nd: Trump says Iran war may last more than 4 or 5 weeks, Hegseth briefs reporters on war aims; Global energy markets spike; FBI investigates whether Texas bar shooting was an act of terrorism.

video:video.video:title: Austin police release bodycam videos of officers locating shooting suspect
video:video.video:description: The 53 year old suspect, who was killed by police, is seen holding an AR-15 rifle on a video captured by a nearby parked vehicle.

video:video.video:title: Michelle Williams wins Actor Award
video:video.video:description: Michelle Williams says the "Dying for Sex" cast will "always be connected." She won the Actor Award for her lead role in the Hulu and Disney+ limited series.

video:video.video:title: Millions left without power after major blackout hits Cuba's western region
video:video.video:description: A blackout has hit the western half of Cuba. Millions of people in Havana and beyond were without power on Wednesday in the latest outage to affect an island struggling with dwindling oil reserves and a crumbling electric grid.

video:video.video:title: US-Israeli bombardment of Iran intensifies, as Tehran presses attacks across the region
video:video.video:description: Israel stepped up attacks on Iranian missile launchers and weapons factories, while Iran fired dozens of ballistic missiles at Israel and launched drone attacks across the Gulf.

video:video.video:title: Protesters in Portugal call for more affordable housing
video:video.video:description: Protesters took to the streets in cities across Portugal to call for more affordable housing saying they are feeling increasingly suffocated by rising rent prices and unaffordable homes.

video:video.video:title: Israeli airstrikes hit Beirut and thousands seek shelter
video:video.video:description: Lebanese civilians have been fleeing areas targeted by Israel, that carried out dozens of airstrikes throughout the day, hitting the capital's southern suburbs as well as areas in southern and eastern Lebanon.

video:video.video:title: Iran's UN envoy blasts US 'hypocrisy' over attacks
video:video.video:description: Amir Saeid Iravani on Monday said the United States was guilty of war crimes against his nation. Addressing reporters in New York, Iravani accused the United States of hypocrisy.

video:video.video:title: Trump says 'worst case' scenario in Iran would be a new leader as bad as the Ayatollah
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said the "worst case" scenario in Iran would be if the nation's next leader is "as bad" as the late Ayatollah Khamenei.

video:video.video:title: EU wants to know more before agreeing to Trump's Hormuz warship demands
video:video.video:description: European countries on Monday demanded to know more about U.S. President Donald Trump's plans for the war on Iran and when the conflict might end as they weighed whether to agree to his call to send warships to help shore up security in the Persian Gulf.

video:video.video:title: Pope expresses deep concern about strikes on Iran
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV said Sunday he was “profoundly concerned” about the U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran and urged both sides to “stop the spiral of violence before it becomes an irreparable abyss.”

video:video.video:title: From mini-sabbaticals to adult gap years, more people are taking extended work breaks
video:video.video:description: From mini-sabbaticals to adult gap years, some workers are stepping away from their jobs for months to travel, pursue passions or recover from burnout.

video:video.video:title: Each spring a rural corner of Catalonia turns pink as peach and nectarine trees blossom
video:video.video:description: Local officials say around 20,000 people visit the orchards near the village of Aitona in the Catalonia region, in northeastern Spain, each year during the brief flowering season of peach and nectarine trees.

video:video.video:title: Selma commemorates Bloody Sunday as new voting rights concerns arise
video:video.video:description: Sixty-one years after state troopers attacked Civil Rights marchers on the Edmund Pettus Bridge in Selma, thousands gathered in the Alabama city on Sunday, amid new concerns about the future of the Voting Rights Act.

video:video.video:title: Suspected meteor falling over Cleveland could be seen from far away
video:video.video:description: A suspected meteor that fell from the sky over the Cleveland area shook homes and startled residents who heard a boom that some compared to an explosion.

video:video.video:title: Zelenskyy offers countries assistance to combat Iranian drones he says Russia uses against Ukraine
video:video.video:description: Volodymyr Zelenskyy said on Friday that Ukraine has received requests from six countries for drone combat assistance.

video:video.video:title: Witness video shows chaotic moments of Texas bar shooting
video:video.video:description: A gunman wearing clothes with an Iranian flag design and the words “Property of Allah” killed two people and wounded 14 early Sunday at a Texas bar, a law enforcement official told The Associated Press.

video:video.video:title: Baby Sumatra tiger making good progress at Berlin zoo
video:video.video:description: A Sumatran tiger cub born at Berlin Zoo in early January successfully passed its first veterinary examination on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: On Iran, Trump says 'we're putting an end to all of this threat once and for all'
video:video.video:description: As fuel prices have risen, Trump insisted: “We’re putting an end to all of this threat once and for all," and the result will be lower "oil and gas prices for American families."

video:video.video:title: EU leaders criticize Hungary's Orbán for blocking aid to Ukraine
video:video.video:description: European Union leaders on Thursday lashed out at Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán, accusing him of hijacking critical aid for Ukraine and undermining EU decision-making in an effort to win an election at home next month.

video:video.video:title: Over 1,000 killed in Israel-Hezbollah war, AP Explains
video:video.video:description: Israeli strikes against the Iranian-backed Hezbollah militant group in Lebanon have displaced more than 1 million people — roughly 20% of the population — according to the Lebanese government, which says more than 1,000 people have been killed.

video:video.video:title: ABC cancels ‘Bachelorette’ season with Taylor Frankie Paul
video:video.video:description: ABC has canceled the upcoming season of “The Bachelorette,” starring Taylor Frankie Paul, citing a newly released video from 2023 of an altercation between Paul and her ex-boyfreind Dakota Mortensen.

video:video.video:title: Jamie Lee Curtis, Nicole Kidman's friendship brought 'Scarpetta' to life
video:video.video:description: Jamie Lee Curtis and Nicole Kidman have brought "Scarpetta," the popular book series from Patricia Cornwell, to the screen after decades of attempts from Hollywood.

video:video.video:title: French ex-president in court for first day of appeals trial over conspiracy to finance campaign
video:video.video:description: Nicolas Sarkozy arrived in court on Monday for the first day of his appeals trial over a criminal conspiracy in a scheme to finance his 2007 election campaign with funds from Libya.

video:video.video:title: Sheryl Lee Ralph: 'I'm blessed beyond compare'
video:video.video:description: Sheryl Lee Ralph says she is "blessed beyond compare" after she was named Time Magazine Woman of the Year.

video:video.video:title: Eddie Vedder says more Pearl Jam music is on the way
video:video.video:description: Eddie Vedder says that after 35 years, Pearl Jam is "more of a group now than ever" and fans can expect new music.

video:video.video:title: US envoy Waltz defends attack on Iran at emergency Security Council meeting
video:video.video:description: His remarks come as the United Nations chief Antonio Guterres condemned the U.S.-Israeli airstrikes on Iran and called for an immediate return to negotiations “to pull the region, and our world, back from the brink.”

video:video.video:title: Hamas says officers killed in Israeli strike on police vehicle on south-north Salah al-Din route in Gaza
video:video.video:description: At least 12 Palestinians, including two boys, a pregnant woman and eight police officers, were killed Sunday by Israeli airstrikes in the war-torn Gaza Strip, hospital authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Protesters clash with anti-riot police near US embassy in Baghdad over killing of Khamenei
video:video.video:description: Journalists of The Associated Press saw dozens of people attempting to cross a bridge leading to Baghdad’s heavily fortified Green Zone, which houses Iraqi government buildings and the sprawling U.S.

video:video.video:title: Iranian state TV says son of late supreme leader has been named his successor
video:video.video:description: Iranian state TV on Sunday announced that Mojtaba Khamenei, the son of the late supreme leader, had been named his successor.

video:video.video:title: Plane crashes into Phoenix homes
video:video.video:description: A home security camera captured the moment a small plane crashed into two homes in Phoenix on Wednesday morning, injuring three people.

video:video.video:title: Former FBI Director Robert Mueller, who investigated Russia-Trump campaign ties, dies
video:video.video:description: Robert S. Mueller III, the FBI director who later became special counsel in charge of investigating ties between Russia and Donald Trump’s presidential campaign, has died.

video:video.video:title: From needlepoint to mahjong, young people are embracing ‘grandma hobbies’
video:video.video:description: Droves of young people are turning to tactile, analog hobbies and activities, such as needlework and mahjong, as a means of escaping technology and reconnecting with creativity and exploration.

video:video.video:title: Dog rescued from elevated train platform in Philadelphia
video:video.video:description: A dog is safe after a rescue from an elevated train platform in Philadelphia. Stranded on a narrow ledge high above around, the dog was saved by ACCT Philly with help from police and transit workers.

video:video.video:title: Poland transforms from post-communist wreck to European growth champion in 35 years
video:video.video:description: Poland's economy has been transformed dramatically since the fall of communism in 1989. Once struggling, it is now one of Europe's most successful.

video:video.video:title: Air raid siren sound in Tel Aviv as Iranian missiles launch towards Israel
video:video.video:description: Air raid sirens sounded and interceptions were seen across Tel Aviv on Tuesday as Iranian missiles were launched towards Israel.

video:video.video:title: Ohio State names provost as its new president after predecessor's abrupt resignation
video:video.video:description: Ohio State has moved past the abrupt resignation of the university’s president by elevating its chief academic officer into the role.

video:video.video:title: Canada and Australia leaders urge war de-escalation, but agree Iran can't get nuclear weapons
video:video.video:description: Canadian and Australian prime ministers have called for a de-escalation of the Iran war but added the Iranians must never gain a nuclear weapon.

video:video.video:title: Decades of abuse by Catholic priests in Rhode Island revealed in sprawling investigation
video:video.video:description: A new report says Rhode Island Catholic clergy abused hundreds of children for decades, while church leaders worked to downplay and minimize the scandal.

video:video.video:title: Why some airports might replace TSA agents with private contractors
video:video.video:description: As TSA staffing shortages cause long airport lines nationwide, San Francisco International Airport’s private security model is drawing attention in a national debate.

video:video.video:title: Apple’s 'big week' starts with a pair of $599 devices aimed at budget buyers
video:video.video:description: Apple’s new budget-friendly iPhone 17e, a new entry-level Macbook Neo, updated iPad Air models, refreshed monitors and higher-end chipsets were all on display at a hands-on media event in New York.

video:video.video:title: An inside look at the Oscars Rolex Greenroom
video:video.video:description: Before and after hitting the Academy Awards stage, Hollywood's biggest stars can be found chatting backstage in the Oscars Greenroom hosted by watch maker Rolex.

video:video.video:title: Key moments from Bill Clinton's testimony on Jeffrey Epstein
video:video.video:description: Videos of former President Bill Clinton and former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton answering questions about convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein have been released by a House committee investigating the late financier.

video:video.video:title: Triceratops skeleton 'Trey' is headed to auction in a hot market
video:video.video:description: A triceratops skeleton that once greeted visitors at a Wyoming museum is heading to an online auction as dinosaur prices keep soaring.

video:video.video:title: Coco Jones reveals what Ciara taught her
video:video.video:description: At an Essence Black Women in Hollywood event in Los Angeles, Coco Jones revealed that her best mentor has been Ciara.

video:video.video:title: Major historical documents arrive in Kansas City as part of nation's 250th anniversary celebration
video:video.video:description: Some of the United States' most important historical documents arrived in Kansas City Monday as part of the country's 250th anniversary commemoration (AP video by Nick Ingram)

video:video.video:title: Paxton, headed to US Senate primary runoff, says ‘Texas is not for sale’
video:video.video:description: Longtime Sen. John Cornyn and MAGA favorite Ken Paxton are heading to a May runoff in Texas’ Republican Senate primary, setting up what's expected to be a nasty and expensive second round and a renewed push to win the endorsement of President Donald Trump.

video:video.video:title: Travelers experience shorter waiting times than expected at some US airports
video:video.video:description: Flying during the ongoing government shutdown has become a guessing game for wait times, with the daily attendance of Transportation Security Administration officers determining whether security lines move quickly or stretch far outside the terminal.

video:video.video:title: Reindeer racing thrills spectators in Finland's frigid north near Russian border
video:video.video:description: Traditional reindeer racing that's been delighting spectators for decades in Finland brought hundreds of fans to the Salla Reindeer Cup in the frigid town of Salla this past weekend.

video:video.video:title: Valencia burns effigies as Las Fallas fire and firework festival comes to an end
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of painstakingly crafted wood and papier-maché sculptures were burned during the festival of Las Fallas, the most important yearly celebration in Valencia, on Thursday.

video:video.video:title: Breast cancer survivor knits prostheses in Kenya instead of costly silicone
video:video.video:description: A Kenyan woman who survived breast cancer is knitting prostheses and training others to make them in a country where silicone ones are expensive.

video:video.video:title: Distraught relatives mourn women and child killed in collapse of wall on tents for displaced
video:video.video:description: The three-meter-high wall collapsed over tents sheltering displaced people in Khan Younis in the Gaza Strip, the city’s Nasser Hospital said.

video:video.video:title: Best actor nominee Timothée Chalamet arrives at the Oscars
video:video.video:description: Best actor nominee Timothée Chalamet arrives at the 98th Academy Awards. He is nominated for his role in “Marty Supreme.”

video:video.video:title: Baghdad residents express concern about economic impact of continuing Iran war
video:video.video:description: Residents in Baghdad expressed concerns about the prolongation of the U.S.-Israel war against Iran and its security and economic consequences on Iraq.

video:video.video:title: Egypt's President meets King Hamad bin Isa Al Khalifa in Manama during Gulf tour
video:video.video:description: Egypt's President Abdel-Fattah el-Sissi on Saturday met with the King of Bahrain in Manama to discuss the escalating conflict in the region.

video:video.video:title: 50 years after Argentina’s bloody coup, families still search for the disappeared
video:video.video:description: Nearly 50 years after the Argentina's 1976 coup that led to a repressive dictatorship, families are still searching for and burying the disappeared.

video:video.video:title: Dog rescued from elevated train platform in Philadelphia
video:video.video:description: ACCT Philly, the city’s open-intake shelter, received a call Sunday about a dog stranded high above the ground on a SEPTA train platform.

video:video.video:title: Iranian attack triggers huge blaze at Bahrain’s Muharraq government facility
video:video.video:description: Bahrain Interior ministry released a video of a huge fire after Iranian attacks at a facility in Muharraq Governorate, northeast of the country’s capital, Manama.

video:video.video:title: Iran’s president threatens more attacks on US targets
video:video.video:description: Iran’s president has threatened to step up attacks on American targets throughout the Middle East as the U.S. and Israel press ahead with their air campaign.

video:video.video:title: North Koreans take part in parliament election, seven years since last vote
video:video.video:description: The election is for the North's parliament, officially called the Supreme People's Assembly, which has more than 600 deputies from all over the country.

video:video.video:title: Roy Cooper speaks after North Carolina Senate Democratic nomination win
video:video.video:description: Former North Carolina Gov. Roy Cooper won the Democratic nomination for U.S. Senate and will face Republican Michael Whatley in the fall for a seat currently held by retiring Sen.

video:video.video:title: Governor Abbott and prominent Texas CEOs attend ceremony launching debut of NASDAQ Texas
video:video.video:description: Nasdaq Texas is structured as a dual listing venue, meaning companies can list on the Texas-based exchange while remaining part of Nasdaq’s broader national and global marketplace.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories March 16
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Monday, March 16th: Trump calls on allies to help reopen Strait of Hormuz; Israeli rescue services say seven wounded in fire after sirens warned of combined rocket and drone attack; Sean Penn meets with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy; 200 trainees poised to be first gr

video:video.video:title: Iran has not requested talks with the US to end the war, Iran's ambassador to Egypt says
video:video.video:description: Iran has not requested talks with the United States to bring an end to the widening war, Iran’s ambassador to Egypt told AP Thursday, denying comments by US President Donald Trump that the country “wants to talk.”

video:video.video:title: How Poland moved from poor to prosperous in just 35 years
video:video.video:description: Poland is now among the world's 20 largest economies. This is how it happened.

video:video.video:title: Hawaii's Kilauea volcano erupts, shooting towering lava fountains
video:video.video:description: Hawaii's Kilauea volcano is shooting lava fountains up to 1,000 feet, and falling ash and volcanic fragments are forcing temporary closures on the Big Island.

video:video.video:title: Israel orders destruction of all bridges over Lebanon’s Litani River
video:video.video:description: Israeli Defense Minister, Israel Katz, expanded the military’s list of targets in Lebanon to include all bridges over the country’s Litani River, a focal point of the renewed Israel-Hezbollah conflict.

video:video.video:title: Displaced residents of Beirut's southern suburbs comment after Israeli military evacuation notice
video:video.video:description: Thousands of people displaced from Beirut's southern suburbs filled the streets and public squares of the Lebanese capital.

video:video.video:title: TSA Union calls for Congress to end shutdown
video:video.video:description: Travelers are planning to arrive earlier at the airport to make up for longer TSA lines as the TSA Union calls for Congress to end the shutdown.

video:video.video:title: Israeli group discreetly organized mystery flights evacuating Palestinians from Gaza - AP Explains
video:video.video:description: The plane carrying about 150 Palestinians from Gaza came as a surprise to everyone on the ground when it landed in South Africa in November. It wasn't the only one.

video:video.video:title: Rand Paul confronts DHS nominee Mullin in Senate hearing
video:video.video:description: Markwayne Mullin faced questions Wednesday from the Senate Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs Committee.

video:video.video:title: Senators head toward vote on President Donald Trump’s decision to embark on a war against Iran
video:video.video:description: The vote is an extraordinary test in Congress for a conflict that has rapidly spread across the Middle East with no clear U.S. exit strategy.

video:video.video:title: Market swings? Advisors say stick to your plan, not your emotions
video:video.video:description: When stock markets are falling, it’s natural to want to do something quickly to protect your savings. Historically, though, staying calm has been best when investing.

video:video.video:title: Michael B Jordan on Oscars Red Carpet
video:video.video:description: Michael B. Jordan, best actor nominee, arrives on the red carpet at the 98th Academy Awards. He is nominated for his role as twins in “Sinners.”

video:video.video:title: AP reporters add context to Trump's reasons for attacking Iran
video:video.video:description: The Trump administration has been trying to explain why it struck Iran and killed its supreme leader, and the shifting reasons range from Iran’s nuclear program to its ballistic missiles capabilities.

video:video.video:title: Maggie Gyllenhaal unleashes 'The Bride!' in New York
video:video.video:description: "The Bride!" stars Jessie Buckley and Christian Bale join filmmaker Maggie Gyllenhaal at the New York premiere of her re-imagining of “The Bride of Frankenstein.”

video:video.video:title: UK warship departs for Cyprus amid Iran war
video:video.video:description: The U.K. warship HMS Dragon set sail from Portsmouth on Tuesday and is on its way to Cyprus. The 200-strong crew could be seen standing onboard the Royal Navy destroyer as the ship left the dock.

video:video.video:title: Citing 'tremendous progress,' Trump gives the US a 15 out of 10 grade for Iran war
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump says he gives the U.S. a 15 out of 10 grade for the Iran war. The president opened his remarks, at an unrelated White House event on AI, saying that the U.S. is “doing very well on the war front, to put it mildly.”

video:video.video:title: Travel insiders give advice amidst Iran war travel chaos
video:video.video:description: The Iran war continues to upended travel across the Middle East and beyond. After almost a week of conflict, airspace closures and flight cancelations, travel industry insiders recommend postponing unnecessary travel if possible, checking refund or insurance policies and, most importantly, monitorin

video:video.video:title: Ryan Coogler uplifts educators after Oscar win
video:video.video:description: Oscar winner Ryan Coogler uplifts educators who may incorporate "Sinners" into classes: "I think they'll know how to do it best."

video:video.video:title: Some Indian eateries forced to close due to shortage of cooking gas
video:video.video:description: Restaurant owners in India say they are struggling to secure liquefied petroleum gas cylinders. Much of the gas is imported, traveling through the Strait of Hormuz, which is effectively closed due to the Iran war.

video:video.video:title: A global party begins: BTS relaunches with a massive Seoul concert
video:video.video:description: BTS will stage its long-awaited comeback concert on Saturday night in Seoul. In a free concert expected to draw tens of thousands of fans, the K-pop juggernaut's seven members will perform songs from their first album in nearly four years, “ARIRANG.”

video:video.video:title: Mojtaba Khamenei chosen to succeed his father as supreme leader of Iran
video:video.video:description: Mojtaba Khamenei, a son of Iran’s late supreme leader, has been named his successor. He had long been considered a contender, even before an Israeli strike killed his father at the start of the war.

video:video.video:title: Proud, ‘Imperfect Women’ star in new thriller

video:video.video:description: Italy’s conservative premier, Giorgia Meloni, faces a pivotal political test in a two-day referendum on judicial reform that started on Sunday, a vote that has transformed into a broader judgment on her leadership at home and abroad.

video:video.video:description: The U.S. counterterrorism official who resigned Tuesday had been a staunch supporter of President Donald Trump through his 2020 election defeat, the Jan. 6 riots and years of conservative media advocacy and failed congressional bids.

video:video.video:description: AP video shows apparent interceptions in the sky, after the sound of an explosion was heard in Doha, Qatar's capital.

video:video.video:description: San Francisco is having an unusual March heat wave, with temperatures nearing 90 degrees, marking the city’s hottest start to spring in nearly 20 years.

video:video.video:description: A U.N country representative in Afghanistan on Tuesday expressed its "deepest condolences" to the families of those killed by an airstrike on a drug treatment hospital in Afghanistan.

video:video.video:description: At a special London screening for her latest film "Ready or Not: Here I Come," Sarah Michelle Gellar says she's been very moved by the fans' reaction to the news of the "Buffy" revival being cancelled.

video:video.video:description: TSA agents are facing financial pressures during an ongoing government funding lapse — the third shutdown in fewer than six months that has forced the officers who screen airport passengers and luggage to keep working without pay.

video:video.video:description: Actor Joel Edgerton talks global issues on Oscars red carpet.

video:video.video:description: The bus was returning after the pilgrims visited the revered Manakamana Temple, a popular Hindu temple where devotees believe a goddess will grant them their wish if they visit the shrine.

video:video.video:title: Budapest's vintage 'freight trams' celebrate 100 years in transit service
video:video.video:description: The vintage machines were introduced after World War I damaged much of the city's infrastructure. Known locally as mukis, they run on electricity provided by overhead wires and travel on Budapest's vast tram rail network, which is widely regarded as one of the busiest.

video:video.video:description: Serial killers have always intrigued audiences, with their atrocious crimes told in documentaries, podcasts, and TV shows.

video:video.video:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth says Iran is “exercising sheer desperation” in the Strait of Hormuz, which the war has effectively closed, blocking a significant portion of the world’s oil.

video:video.video:description: The airport has repeatedly been targeted by Iranian drones, causing interruptions to air travel. It has also been shut down for several days since the start of the war between US-Israel and Iran on February 28.

video:video.video:description: Dozens of French travelers who were stranded in the Middle East landed in Paris on Wednesday as travel across the region remained heavily disrupted by the widening Iran war.

video:video.video:description: Advocates hope a growing unhappiness over wage and wealth inequalities that have increased since the COVID-19 pandemic will lead more U.S. states to adopt policies raising taxes on the wealthy.

video:video.video:title: On International Women’s Day, women in Gaza say they are suffering with no end in sight
video:video.video:description: Palestinian women in Gaza reminisced about their livelihood before the war. The majority of them live starkly different lives in tents that do little to protect them from winter rains and the scorching summer heat, and spend hours every day in pursuit of basic necessities.

video:video.video:description: A hospital and tourist site in southwestern Iran have been damaged from U.S. or Israeli strikes, killing at least one child, according to Iranian news agencies.

video:video.video:description: How do you want to die? At the Death Fest fair in Thailand, visitors are encouraged to plan for end of life. (AP video shot by: Ed Peerayot)

video:video.video:description: NAACP President Derrick Johnson explains what he thinks accountability would look like after a racial slur was shouted at the BAFTA Awards while Michael B.

video:video.video:title: Iranian missile hits Holon in central Israel with no reported casualties, AP explains
video:video.video:description: An Iranian missile struck a site in central Israel Friday evening. There were no casualties recorded as a result of the attacks throughout the day, according to authorities.

video:video.video:description: The Chinese company AutoFlight, founded in 2017, developed the Matrix, which can carry up to 10 passengers.

video:video.video:description: On Tuesday, oil prices rose again and raised more alarms. The price for a barrel of Brent crude, the international standard, briefly leaped above $84.

video:video.video:title: Senegal stripped of AFCON title, sparking disbelief and an appeal - AP dispatch
video:video.video:description: Soccer fans in Dakar were in disbelief after Senegal was stripped of its Africa Cup of Nations title, two months after the national team returned to the capital to a hero’s welcome following a dramatic win in Morocco.

video:video.video:title: ROTC students at Old Dominion subdued and killed the shooter who killed 1 person, wounded 2
video:video.video:description: The FBI says ROTC students in an Old Dominion University classroom subdued and killed the shooter who killed one person and wounded two other.

video:video.video:description: Rescue crews were still digging bodies out of the rubble of a drug rehabilitation hospital in the Afghan capital Tuesday morning, after officials there said an overnight Pakistani airstrike killed at least 400 people at the facility.

video:video.video:description: Residents of Irbil, the capital of the semi-autonomous Kurdish region in northern Iraq, are increasingly being caught in the crossfire of the war in the Middle East triggered by the attacks launched by the U.S. and Israel on Iran.

video:video.video:title: Challenging your brain helps keep it healthy. Here's how to do it
video:video.video:description: Research increasingly shows a variety of habits and hobbies offer a helpful cognitive workout. One recent study linked lifelong learning — things like reading, learning another language, playing chess — to slower cognitive decline, even postponing Alzheimer's for a few years.

video:video.video:description: In just 10 days, more than 800,000 people in Lebanon have been displaced by war, just over a year since the last conflict uprooted over a million from their homes.

video:video.video:title: Former Phoenix city councilor who worked with Chavez reacts to sex abuse allegations
video:video.video:description: Former Phoenix City Council member Mary Rose Wilcox, who marched alongside César Chavez at protests in the 1970s, said she is dismayed at the sex abuse allegations against the long-admired Latino icon.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories March 18
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Wednesday, March 18th: Mullin urges Congress to fund DHS; Iran's natural gas facilities attacked; US stocks fall as Brent crude jumps toward $110 per barrel; Afghanistan and Pakistan call a temporary ceasefire for the Muslim holiday.

video:video.video:title: Dozens of Christian Lebanese gather for funeral of man killed in Israeli strike
video:video.video:description: The United Nations peacekeeping mission in southern Lebanon, known as UNIFIL, helped almost 100 residents leave Alma al-Shaab early Tuesday, the last group of residents who for days tried to stand their ground amid the ongoing bombardments between Israel and the Hezbollah militant group.

video:video.video:title: Fires send huge clouds of smoke into Tehran night sky after barrage of Israeli airstrikes
video:video.video:description: Pillars of flame rose above an oil storage facility in Tehran late on Saturday as Israel carried out further air strikes.

video:video.video:title: Hot-air balloon crashes into Texas communications tower
video:video.video:description: Crews in East Texas rescued two people stranded hundreds of feet in the air after a hot-air balloon became entangled on a communications tower over the weekend.

video:video.video:title: Los Angeles residents call for renaming César Chavez street signs following abuse allegations
video:video.video:description: California Rising demands the renaming of streets named after César Chavez due to allegations of sexual abuse during his time as president of the United Farm Workers union.

video:video.video:description: The estimated wait time at the standard security checkpoint at the William P. Hobby Airport in Houston early Sunday evening was at one point three hours, according to the Houston Airports website.

video:video.video:title: Russian attack reported in Mykolaiv causing deaths and damage
video:video.video:description: The Ukrainian city of Mykolaiv was targeted in a Russian drone attack on Wednesday, two local officials said.

video:video.video:description: The Israeli military said on Tuesday soldiers were “operating in southern Lebanon’ as it continues strikes against Hezbollah.

video:video.video:description: At a news conference organized by law enforcement and faith leaders, Rabbi Josh Bennett said the Temple Israel congregation will return to its house of worship in West Bloomfield Township.

video:video.video:title: Celebrations in Caracas as Venezuela beats US to win first World Baseball Classic title
video:video.video:description: Venezuela won the World Baseball Classic for the first time, rebounding from a blown eighth-inning lead to beat the United States 3-2 Tuesday night on Eugenio Suárez’s tiebreaking double in the ninth.

video:video.video:title: Two high profile real estate brokers and their brother convicted drug, rape charges, AP explains
video:video.video:description: Oren, Alon and Tal Alexander have been convicted of charges accusing them of conspiring to drug and rape multiple women.

video:video.video:title: Mary J. Blige celebrates her journey: 'It's uplifting to have a career like this'
