video:video.video:description: Elon Musk is accused of making false and misleading statements that drove down Twitter’s stock price ahead of his purchase of the social media platform in 2022.

video:video.video:title: Videos show aftermath of strikes on Iran's cyber police and space research center
video:video.video:description: The strikes on Sunday happened as Iran said US and Israeli strikes damaged about 43,000 residential and commercial units.

video:video.video:title: Strong winds cause property damage in the Detroit metropolitan area
video:video.video:description: Strong winds cause property damage in the Detroit metropolitan area

video:video.video:title: Sky is lit up by airstrikes in eastern Tehran as evening falls
video:video.video:description: The U.S. hammered Iran for a sixth day as the conflict in the Middle East continued to escalate on Thursday.

video:video.video:title: Brash, bare-knuckle-fighting Sherlock Holmes hits small screens
video:video.video:description: Hero Fiennes Tiffin plays a young Sherlock Holmes in Guy Ritchie's rip-roaring, explosive reimaging of Arthur Conan Doyle's fictional sleuth - with added fight scenes.

video:video.video:title: Wall Street holds steadier as oil prices stop spiking, for now at least
video:video.video:description: The U.S. stock market is holding steadier, for now at least, following two days of punishing swings driven by worries about how high oil prices will go because of the war with Iran.

video:video.video:title: Several killed, others missing after massive avalanche of garbage at Indonesia’s largest landfill
video:video.video:description: Rescuers are continuing to search for several people reported missing after a deadly avalanche of garbage at Indonesia’s largest landfill.

video:video.video:title: At least 7 wounded after rocket attack on northern Israel
video:video.video:description: At least seven people, including two teenage girls, were wounded after a rocket strike set two homes ablaze in Nahariya in northern Israel, Israeli rescue services said.

video:video.video:title: A Connecticut fire department is rolling out a new dispatch system that aims to ease stress
video:video.video:description: Danbury, Connecticut has joined hundreds of other US cities that have installed new firehouse alarm systems aimed at reducing firefighters' stress when calls come in.

video:video.video:title: Residents and emergency workers clear strike damage in Tehran's Javadieh neighborhood
video:video.video:description: The strike hit a police station in the area of Javadieh and some surrounding buildings were damaged.

video:video.video:title: What to know about the strikes on a British base in Cyprus
video:video.video:description: U.K. officials said an attack drone hit the runway at RAF Akrotiri, a British air force base in Cyprus, late Sunday.

video:video.video:title: 23 dead, flights disrupted and military deployed after heavy rains pound Kenya's capital overnight
video:video.video:description: Twenty-three people died, flights were disrupted and the military was deployed as motorists in the Kenyan capital were stranded for hours on Saturday after roads were flooded following heavy rains overnight.

video:video.video:title: A record number of political parties register for Haiti's first election in a decade
video:video.video:description: A record 280 political parties and counting had registered by Thursday’s deadline to participate in Haiti’s first general election in a decade, although not all will make the cut.

video:video.video:title: Video shows amur leopard cat stealing fish from otter
video:video.video:description: An Amur leopard cat steals fish from an otter in a video captured at a Russian Nature Reserve.

video:video.video:title: Explosions and flames seen in southern Tehran following new airstrikes
video:video.video:description: Several airstrikes hit neighborhoods in Tehran’s east and southwest Saturday night as Israel’s military confirmed a new wave of attacks. They came after U.S.

video:video.video:title: Rescue workers remove rubble after a building in Isfahan is hit by US-Israeli strike
video:video.video:description: Iranian state television broadcast footage Friday on its website showing damage to buildings and cars following a U.S.

video:video.video:title: Bark and Ride: The canine coach exclusively for dogs
video:video.video:description: A canine coach exclusively for dogs is gaining celebrity status in the north of England, U.K. Billy's Bus is part of a dog walking service that picks up your pooch for a luxury ride before taking them out for a run in the countryside.

video:video.video:title: How Ukraine's front line became a laboratory for drone innovation
video:video.video:description: The night air in eastern Ukraine is crisp, and a myriad of stars scatter above a small crew of soldiers watching for Iranian-designed Shahed drones that Russia launches in waves.

video:video.video:title: Homes damaged by falling debris after Pakistan says it intercepted drones launched by Taliban
video:video.video:description: Drone attacks on Pakistan intercepted by the country's military on Friday injured two children in the city of Quetta and damaged homes.

video:video.video:title: Trump says a former president had an Iran confession; aides to his predecessors deny recent contact
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said he spoke to a predecessor who had expressed regret to him about not attacking Iran when they occupied the Oval Office.

video:video.video:title: Rose Byrne says nailing the hair and makeup to play a real unhoused woman fighting a giant unfair towing bill helped honor her character in 'Tow'
video:video.video:description: Rose Byrne stars in the new film, "Tow," based on the true story of an unhoused Seattle woman living in her car, who was slapped with an unfair towing bill for her stolen car, that added up to $21,634.

video:video.video:title: Hezbollah leader vows to keep fighting Israel
video:video.video:description: In his first public address since joining the war, Hezbollah leader Naim Kassem said the Lebanese militant group rejects the government’s plan to disarm its fighters.

video:video.video:title: B2K, Bow Wow’s ‘Boys 4 Life’ tour promotes the power of brotherhood
video:video.video:description: The platinum-selling R&B group B2K has reunited for the upcoming “Boys 4 Life” tour, and they’re co-headlining with fellow 2000s superstar, Bow Wow.

video:video.video:title: Two men found with hundreds of ants are charged with illegally dealing in wildlife in Kenya
video:video.video:description: A Chinese national and a Kenyan man were charged Tuesday with unlawfully dealing in wildlife species after they were found in possession of hundreds of live ants stored in specialized tubes.

video:video.video:title: Argentinian winemakers move away from chemicals towards organic wine
video:video.video:description: The grape harvest is underway in the Mendoza region of Argentina. In the Uco Valley, some producers have chosen to grow their grapes in closer harmony with the natural environment to encourage biodiversity.

video:video.video:title: Candidates cast their votes in the second round of French municipal elections
video:video.video:description: French voters returned to the polls Sunday for the second and final round of municipal elections in over 1,500 communes.

video:video.video:title: America's official mammal, the bison, gets a bronze tribute for the country's 250th birthday
video:video.video:description: Three bison statues cast in bronze have taken up a permanent display outside the Smithsonian’s National Museum of Natural History in Washington.

video:video.video:title: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy calls on UK parliament to help build up anti-drone defences
video:video.video:description: In a speech to members of the British Parliament, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy called for joint efforts in building up anti-drone defenses amid the war in Iran.

video:video.video:title: Lebanese village wakes up to destruction after 16 killed in night of Israeli airstrikes
video:video.video:description: Residents of a town in Lebanon’s eastern Bekaa Valley walked among the rubble and looked in disbelief Saturday at the vast destruction from overnight clashes that left 16 dead and 35 wounded, according to Lebanon's Health Ministry.

video:video.video:title: Noah Wyle says it's 'odd' being celebratory when 'the rest of the world is suffering'
video:video.video:description: Noah Wyle says it's an "odd disconnect" to be in a celebratory mood during awards season while "innocent people are suffering."

video:video.video:title: Fire engulfs warehouse in Florida
video:video.video:description: Firefighters battle a massive warehouse fire near the Florida Turnpike in Miami-Dade County

video:video.video:title: Ukrainian President Zelenskyy arrives in Spain
video:video.video:description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy arrived in Madrid late on Tuesday as part of his European tour.

video:video.video:title: Missile strike in Tel Aviv leaves one dead and dozens wounded
video:video.video:description: The U.S. and Israel attacked Iran on Saturday in a massive operation that killed Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and hit government and military sites across the country.

video:video.video:title: A new recruit joins a team of bloodhounds built for searches
video:video.video:description: The North Dakota Highway Patrol's newest recruit has floppy ears, four legs and will be a powerful tool for finding people.

video:video.video:title: Video shows plume of smoke rising at Abu Dhabi’s Port Zayed
video:video.video:description: The French defense minister said a drone strike damaged Sunday a hangar at a French naval base at the port of Abu Dhabi.

video:video.video:title: Small plane flips during takeoff in California
video:video.video:description: There were no injuries reported and the cause of the crash remains unclear, ABC affiliate KABC reported.

video:video.video:title: Trump says NATO is making a ‘very foolish mistake’ by not assisting US in Iran war
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump complains about NATO’s refusal to join the U.S. in the war, calling the decision “disappointing” and bad for the “partnership.”

video:video.video:title: Explosion and plumes of smoke rising in Beirut following Israeli strike
video:video.video:description: An explosion was heard and large plumes of smoke were seen rising in Beirut's southern suburbs on Friday after Israeli airstrikes pounded the capitals of Iran and Lebanon as the U.S. apparently struck an Iranian drone carrier at sea, intensifying its campaign targeting the Islamic Republic’s fleet o

video:video.video:title: Decades of abuse by Catholic priests in Rhode Island revealed in investigation
video:video.video:description: Rhode Island priests preyed on hundreds of children for decades and were protected by bishops more concerned with the church’s reputation than the victims, according to a new report on clergy sexual abuse released by Rhode Island Attorney General Peter Neronha.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories March 5
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Thursday, March 5: Iran and Israel launch more missiles; US submarine sinks Iranian ship; Names of last two U.S. service members killed in Kuwait released; House to hold war powers resolution vote.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories March 3
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for March 3: Iran targets region as US-Israeli assault continues; German chancellor visits White House; Father convicted of 2nd-degree murder for giving school shooting suspect a gun; Lenovo unveils desktop robot.

video:video.video:title: AP video shows smoke rising in Tehran after several US-Israeli strikes on Iran's capital
video:video.video:description: Strikes across Iran's capital Tehran on Monday hit several areas of the city. Israel and the United States are pounding Iran as the war is expanding to several fronts.

video:video.video:title: Oil prices seesaw over uncertainty of war as Iran names new leader - AP explains
video:video.video:description: Oil prices surged and markets slid Monday after hard-line Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei was chosen to succeed his late father as Iran's supreme leader, signalling that Iran was digging in 10 days into the war launched by the United States and Israel.

video:video.video:title: Best actress nominee Jessie Buckley arrives at the Oscars
video:video.video:description: Best actress nominee Jessie Buckley arrives at the 98th Academy Awards. She is nominated for her role in “Hamnet.”

video:video.video:title: Michael B. Jordan wins top honor at Actor Awards
video:video.video:description: “Sinners” star Michael B. Jordan won best male actor in a leading role at the Actor Awards.

video:video.video:title: Ukrainian company showcases interceptor drone designed to stop Shaheds
video:video.video:description: Ukrainian instructors from a private company demonstrated an interceptor drone developed to hunt down Shahed drones Wednesday in the Kyiv region as President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said a Ukrainian team was on its way to Gulf countries to help strengthen defenses as tensions escalate following Iranian

video:video.video:title: Israel fires on Lebanese capital Beirut
video:video.video:description: Israel launched strikes on Lebanon’s capital, Beirut, after the Lebanese militant group Hezbollah fired missiles across the border early Monday.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories March 15
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Sunday, March 15: President Trump appeals to other countries in sending warships to the Strait of Hormuz; Heavy snow in U.S. midwest as forecasters predict storms eastward; Boston holds annual St.

video:video.video:title: Army Staff Sgt. killed in war with Iran returns home to Kentucky in dignified transfer
video:video.video:description: The body of Army Staff Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington, the seventh U.S. service member to die in combat during the U.S. war with Iran, was returned home Friday in preparation for his funeral.

video:video.video:title: 'Sinners' stars reflect on their special Actor Awards moment
video:video.video:description: “Sinners” stars Michael B. Jordan and Delroy Lindo share what it meant to them to have Viola Davis and Samuel L. Jackson present their awards.

video:video.video:title: Pulse nightclub is demolished to make way for a memorial to the 2016 mass shooting
video:video.video:description: Crews began demolishing Pulse nightclub in Orlando, Florida, on Wednesday to make way for a memorial that will pay tribute to the 49 people killed in the attack at the LGBTQ-friendly club nearly a decade ago.

video:video.video:title: Trump administration sues Harvard, saying it violated civil rights law
video:video.video:description: The Justice Department filed a new lawsuit Friday against Harvard University, saying its leadership failed to address antisemitism on campus, creating grounds for the government to freeze existing grants and seek repayment for grants already paid.

video:video.video:title: Ex-Michigan football coach Sherrone Moore reaches plea deal to resolve criminal case
video:video.video:description: Former Michigan football coach Sherrone Moore pleaded no contest to two misdemeanors in a criminal case (AP video: Mike Householder and Larry Lage)

video:video.video:title: UN chief urges diplomatic push to end war in the Middle East
video:video.video:description: The UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres has asserted once more that “the war in the Middle East must stop."

video:video.video:title: Welcoming ceremony for Iran's returning women’s soccer team
video:video.video:description: Members of Iran’s national women’s soccer team were greeted with a welcome ceremony upon their return to the Islamic Republic after several of the players had sought asylum in Australia.

video:video.video:title: Advice for international travelers during the war in the Middle East
video:video.video:description: The U.S. and Israel’s joint war against Iran is upending travel across the Middle East, stranding hundreds of thousands of people.

video:video.video:title: Authorities say ex-NFL player Darron Lee facing murder charge consulted AI bot before calling 911
video:video.video:description: Authorities testified in court that Darron Lee, a former NFL player who is charged with murder in his girlfriend's death in Tennessee, asked an AI bot for advice about the unresponsive woman before calling 911.

video:video.video:title: Sen. Paul says he'll vote against Mullin for DHS, Sen. Fetterman says he's open-minded
video:video.video:description: Republican Senator Rand Paul and Democratic Senator John Fetterman revealed to reporters whether or not they think Markwayne Mullin should be confirmed as the next homeland security secretary, after a hearing on Capitol Hill on Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Israel orders immediate evacuations in southern Lebanon as Beirut strikes intensify - AP Explains
video:video.video:description: Israel has ordered residents of dozens of villages in southern Lebanon to evacuate as airstrikes around Beirut intensify and Hezbollah reports more attacks.

video:video.video:title: Boats capsize off US air base construction site on Okinawa, two dead
video:video.video:description: Two boats carrying 21 people capsized Monday off a controversial relocation site for a U.S. military base off Japan’s southern island of Okinawa.

video:video.video:title: Iranian ambassador to Tunisia says new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei is ‘safe and in good health'
video:video.video:description: Iran’s ambassador to Tunisia mourned Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei at a press conference Thursday and said the new Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, the slain leader’s son, was “safe and in good health” following reports that he had been wounded in a strike at the start of the Iran

video:video.video:title: Hawaii’s worst flooding in 20 years threatens dam, prompts evacuations as more rain looms
video:video.video:description: As Hawaii endures its worst flooding in more than 20 years, officials are urging people in hard-hit areas to evacuate.

video:video.video:title: China to unveil priorities for coming 5 years at major annual political meeting - AP explains
video:video.video:description: China's top political advisory body opened its annual session on Wednesday with a ceremony at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing.
